metadata.title: Political Statements Timeline: Iran-Israel Conflict
metadata.description: A chronological collection of key political statements from world leaders regarding the Iran-Israel conflict, spanning from 2023 through early March 2026.
sources: U.S. Department of Defense Press Releases
Office of the Supreme Leader of Iran (khamenei.ir)
metadata.methodology: Statements were collected from official government communications, press conferences, verified social media accounts, and credible international news sources. Each statement has been reviewed for accuracy with a fact-check rating. All quotes are sourced from verified public records with clickable source links for independent verification.
metadata.disclaimer: All statements are sourced from official government communications, press conferences, and verified news reports. Quotes may be lightly paraphrased for clarity while preserving the speaker's documented position. Click source links to verify each statement independently. Fact-check ratings reflect editorial assessment based on available evidence.

statements.text: The Iran deal was the worst agreement ever negotiated. We got out and the world is safer because of it.
statements.context: Speaking at a campaign rally in Iowa, Trump reiterated his justification for withdrawing the U.S. from the JCPOA in 2018, claiming the decision improved global security.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Trump did withdraw from the JCPOA, multiple intelligence assessments indicated Iran resumed enrichment activities after the withdrawal, arguably reducing safety. The claim that the world is 'safer' is contested by most nonproliferation experts.

statements.text: Nobody wants peace more than me. I would have a deal with Iran done in one week if I were president.
statements.context: During a town hall event, Trump claimed he could rapidly negotiate a new deal with Iran while simultaneously maintaining his stance that maximum pressure was the correct approach.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim of reaching a deal within one week is unsubstantiated and contradicts the complexity of nuclear negotiations, which historically take months or years. No framework for such rapid negotiations was ever presented.

statements.text: Iran understands only strength. When I was president, they didn't dare act up. Now look at the chaos.
statements.context: During a Super Tuesday victory speech, Trump contrasted his administration's approach to Iran with that of the Biden administration, claiming deterrence had collapsed.
statements.factCheckDetail: While some Iranian proxy activity decreased during specific periods, Iran continued enrichment and proxy operations throughout the Trump presidency, including attacks on Saudi oil facilities in 2019. The framing omits significant escalation events like the Soleimani strike aftermath.

statements.text: If Iran does anything to harm Israel, there will be consequences like nobody has ever seen before.
statements.context: In the final weeks of the 2024 presidential campaign, Trump issued a strong warning to Iran following increased tensions in the region around the anniversary of the October 7 attacks.
statements.factCheckDetail: As a candidate at the time, Trump had no authority to enforce this threat. The statement was interpreted as campaign rhetoric rather than a concrete policy commitment.

statements.text: Maximum pressure is back and it's stronger than ever. Iran's economy will collapse if they don't come to the table.
statements.context: In his first major foreign policy address after returning to office, Trump announced the reimposition of broad sanctions on Iran and signaled willingness to negotiate a new deal.
statements.factCheckDetail: While new sanctions were imposed, Iran had already adapted to sanctions through alternative trade networks with China and Russia. Economic analysts noted the impact would be less severe than the first round of maximum pressure due to these workarounds.

statements.text: I told Bibi, you do what you have to do. America stands with Israel, one hundred percent.
statements.context: After reports surfaced of Israeli intelligence indicating accelerated Iranian enrichment, Trump publicly affirmed U.S. support for Israel's security decisions, which critics said amounted to a green light for preemptive strikes.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflected the administration's public posture of unconditional support for Israeli security decisions, as confirmed by multiple White House officials.

statements.text: We don't want war with Iran. Nobody wants war less than me. But if they keep pushing, all options are on the table.
statements.context: Responding to questions about military buildup in the Persian Gulf, Trump attempted to balance his stated desire for peace with warnings about military readiness, a contradiction critics highlighted.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement contradicts concurrent actions including carrier group deployments to the Persian Gulf and expanded military aid to Israel. The phrase 'all options on the table' is traditionally understood as an implicit threat of military force.

statements.text: Iran has been warned. They chose this path. What happens next is on them, not on us.
statements.context: Following the initiation of Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities, Trump framed the escalation as entirely Iran's responsibility, despite criticism that U.S. diplomatic disengagement contributed to the crisis.
statements.factCheckDetail: Attribution of sole responsibility to Iran is disputed. Multiple analysts point to the collapse of diplomatic channels, withdrawal from the JCPOA, and explicit U.S. support for Israeli military action as contributing factors to the escalation.

statements.text: We are working to bring this to a close very quickly. It will be a tremendous outcome, believe me.
statements.context: As the conflict entered its third week with expanding scope, Trump claimed to be personally managing de-escalation efforts, though no concrete diplomatic framework was announced.
statements.factCheckDetail: No evidence of active negotiations or a de-escalation framework was presented. The claim of an imminent resolution contradicted reports from diplomatic sources indicating no back-channel communications were underway.

statements.text: Good reason needed for ground troops, but everything is on the table. We will finish the job.
statements.context: Bloomberg interview suggesting ground invasion possible.

statements.text: Iran has two choices: unconditional surrender or we finish the job. There is no third option.
statements.context: Press conference at White House.

statements.text: We've already won in many ways, but we haven't won enough. The new so-called supreme leader is a lightweight. He's going to have to get approval from us — if he doesn't, he's not going to last long.
statements.context: Phone interview with Times of Israel. Called war a 'short-term excursion' and said ending it would be a 'mutual' decision with Netanyahu. Said he has someone in mind to lead Iran.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim of having 'already won' is premature given Iran continues retaliatory strikes and Hormuz remains closed. The assertion of authority over Iran's leadership selection contradicts international norms of sovereignty.

statements.text: This is a little excursion. It'll be finished pretty quickly. We're doing tremendously well.
statements.context: March 9 press conference. Contradicts Hegseth's signaling of a 4-8 week campaign. Markets remain uncertain about conflict timeline.
statements.factCheckDetail: Characterizing a multi-front war involving 3 carrier groups, 45,000+ deployed troops, 20% of global oil supply disrupted, and 1,300+ confirmed deaths as a 'little excursion' significantly understates the scale and complexity of operations.

statements.text: I think the war is very complete, pretty much. We'll decide together with Israel when it ends. Washington will have the final say.
statements.context: Trump said the war could be over 'pretty quickly' while simultaneously Hegseth warned of the 'most intense day of strikes' yet. Trump said he was 'not happy' with Mojtaba Khamenei and indicated he was open to a Venezuela-style outcome where the regime remains but cooperates.
statements.factCheckDetail: Calling the war 'very complete' contradicts active military operations and Hegseth's announcement of escalated strikes. No ceasefire is in sight and Iran has rejected all mediation attempts.

statements.text: We've destroyed 80 percent of their missile launchers. The war will be over pretty quickly, very soon. But not this week. If they disrupt oil flow any further, we'll increase strikes twentyfold.
statements.context: Trump told Republican Congressional members the war could end 'very soon' while warning Iran against further oil disruption. Called the conflict a 'little excursion.' Oil prices crashed 11-12% on his remarks. Gap widening between US (wanting to wind down) and Israeli (wanting to continue) endgames.
statements.factCheckDetail: 80% of missile launchers claim aligns with CENTCOM's 5,000+ targets struck. However, Iran continues firing projectiles and has rejected all ceasefire overtures. Netanyahu vows 'many surprises' suggesting Israel plans to continue.

statements.text: There's essentially nothing left to target. We've hit 90 percent of their capability. Iran has been set back 50 years.
statements.context: Day 12 comments to reporters. Trump signaled satisfaction with the campaign while Iran deployed Khoramshahr super-heavy missiles — its most powerful conventional weapon — for the first time. 3 more ships attacked at Hormuz. IEA approved record 400M barrel reserve release.
statements.factCheckDetail: While 90% missile reduction aligns with CENTCOM data, Iran demonstrated continued capability by deploying Khoramshahr missiles and attacking 3 more ships. The claim of 'nothing left to target' contradicts ongoing Iranian retaliatory attacks.

statements.text: The war will end soon. There's practically nothing left to target. Anytime I want it to end, it will end.
statements.context: Statement to media. Contradicted by Israeli Defence Minister Katz who said operation has 'no time limit.'
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of 'nothing left to target' contested — Iran retains significant military capability and continues launching missiles. Katz directly contradicted Trump's timeline.

statements.text: The war is going well. We've hit over 6,000 targets. Iran is being set back decades.
statements.context: Day 14 remarks. US claims 6,000+ targets hit, up from 5,000 on Day 13. KC-135 crew all 6 confirmed dead, raising US death toll to 13.
statements.factCheckNote: 6,000+ targets figure reported by NBC. Iran continues retaliatory strikes including cluster missiles that penetrated Israeli defenses.

statements.text: I think he's damaged, but probably alive in some form. The Iranian people should rise up. I'll know when the war is over — I'll feel it in my bones.
statements.context: Trump speaking to Fox News about Mojtaba Khamenei's condition and war timeline. Urged regime change but acknowledged 'very big hurdle.'
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's condition unconfirmed. Hegseth separately said 'wounded and likely disfigured.'

statements.text: CENTCOM has 'totally obliterated every MILITARY target' on Kharg Island. Called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Urged Iranian military to 'lay down their arms, and save what's left of their country.' Warned oil infrastructure is next if Hormuz blockade continues.
statements.context: Truth Social post after Kharg Island bombing raid
statements.factCheckNote: Strike on Kharg Island confirmed. 'Most powerful bombing raid' claim is subjective.

statements.text: We're way ahead of schedule. Iran is totally defeated. They're about to surrender. I've told the G7 — we need China, France, Japan, South Korea, UK to send warships to reopen Hormuz.
statements.context: G7 call and subsequent press remarks; rebuffed ceasefire mediation by Oman and Egypt
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has not signaled surrender. CIA backchannel discussions reported separately suggest ongoing conflict.

statements.text: Iran wants to make a deal, but I'm not ready for one because the terms aren't good enough yet.
statements.context: NBC interview on Day 16 of conflict
statements.factCheckNote: Iran has set specific conditions (guarantees + compensation), not an unconditional desire to 'make a deal' as characterized.

statements.text: The terms are not good enough yet. We may hit Kharg Island a few more times just for fun.
statements.context: Bloomberg interview on Day 16. Trump rejected Iran's ceasefire terms and called on China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the UK to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz to help reopen it.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quotes confirmed by Bloomberg. Call for international naval coalition is a significant escalation in diplomatic pressure.

statements.text: NATO is useless on Hormuz. If they won't help, we'll do it ourselves. Kharg Island seizure is on the table as an economic knockout.
statements.context: FT interview on Day 17. Trump threatened NATO allies over their perceived non-response to Strait of Hormuz disruptions and publicly weighed seizing Iran's Kharg Island, which handles ~90% of Iran's oil exports.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quotes confirmed by NPR and FT reporting. Kharg Island seizure would require major amphibious military operation — unclear if Pentagon has presented such plans.

statements.text: We've gotten some positive response from countries on the Hormuz coalition. But if NATO doesn't help, we may have to rethink what NATO means.
statements.context: Financial Times interview. Also threatened to delay China trip and implied Europe's defense umbrella at risk if warships aren't sent to Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: No country has publicly committed warships. UK, Germany, Australia, Japan have all explicitly declined.

statements.text: Iran's military has been decimated. We've taken out their air force and air defense systems. But I'm not declaring victory yet.
statements.context: Continued mixed messaging — claiming military success while refusing to declare the mission complete.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran continues firing missiles daily. IRGC claims most weapons cache remains intact.

statements.text: I don't think so. But it'll be soon. Won't be long. We're gonna have a much safer world when it's wrapped up. It'll be wrapped up soon.
statements.context: Asked in Oval Office if the war could end this week. Also delayed Xi summit 'a month or so,' said Israel 'would never' use nuclear weapons, and claimed Kharg Island 'literally destroyed' except oil facilities.
statements.factCheckNote: 'Wrapped up soon' contradicts IDF statement of 3+ more weeks of operations through Passover. Kharg Island destruction claim unverified.

statements.text: Israel wouldn't do that. Israel would never do that.
statements.context: Responding to David Sacks warning about Israel potentially using nuclear weapons. Trump said Sacks had not shared that assessment with him.
statements.factCheckNote: Israel maintains a policy of nuclear ambiguity.

statements.text: Scott Bessent is doing a tremendous job. We're opening up the strait for oil. Prices are going to come down very quickly.
statements.context: Commenting on Treasury Secretary Bessent's announcement that US would allow Iranian tankers through Hormuz. Oil dropped 4-6% on the news.
statements.factCheckNote: Oil did drop on the Bessent announcement, but analysts note the strait remains effectively closed to most commercial traffic. Pump price relief likely weeks away.

statements.text: We have totally obliterated their military. Iran is not the same country it was three weeks ago. The war will be wrapped up very soon.
statements.context: Trump claimed Iran's military has been destroyed while delaying his China summit to focus on the war. Pentagon exploring diplomatic off-ramps.
statements.factCheckNote: While significant damage has been inflicted on Iran's military infrastructure, Iran continues to launch missiles and maintain the Hormuz blockade. The claim of total obliteration overstates the situation.

statements.text: Leaving NATO is something to think about. They don't want to help. We do not need the help of anyone. Very bad for the future of NATO.
statements.context: Oval Office remarks alongside Irish PM after NATO allies refused to help secure Strait of Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Trump confirmed remarks to reporters

statements.text: I always thought he was a nice guy. But I always thought he was weak on security, very weak on security.
statements.context: Response to Joe Kent's resignation as NCTC Director
statements.factCheckNote: Trump confirmed response

statements.text: We're knocking them for a loop. They cannot have a nuclear weapon, and they now understand that very strongly. I rebuilt the military in my first term, and I didn't expect to have to use it so much in my second.
statements.context: Evening remarks at the White House alongside Irish PM. Trump praised military success and said Iran now understands it cannot obtain nuclear weapons.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran continues to launch missiles and maintain the Hormuz blockade despite significant damage to its military. The claim of decisive victory overstates the situation.

statements.text: No, I'm not afraid of — I'm really not afraid of anything.
statements.context: When asked if boots on the ground in Iran could become another Vietnam, Trump said he was not afraid. He later added the war would end 'in the very near future' but he wasn't ready to leave yet.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple reporters present. First direct signal Trump is open to ground troops in Iran.

statements.text: We don't know if he's dead or not. A lot of people are saying he's badly disfigured. Nobody's saying he's 100 percent healthy.
statements.context: On Mojtaba Khamenei's status
statements.factCheckNote: No evidence presented for claims about Mojtaba

statements.text: Attended dignified transfer at Dover AFB for 6 service members killed in KC-135 crash. Accompanied by Hegseth, Speaker Johnson, and military families.
statements.context: Dover Air Force Base dignified transfer ceremony
statements.factCheckNote: AP confirmed

statements.text: Israel will NOT again strike the South Pars gas field. But if Iran attacks Qatar again, the United States will massively blow up the entirety of the field.
statements.context: Truth Social post. Axios reports Trump DID coordinate original strike despite denying it.
statements.factCheckNote: Axios: Trump coordinated strike with Netanyahu despite claiming US 'knew nothing'

statements.text: The United States knew nothing about this particular attack on the South Pars Gas Field. If Iran attacks Qatar again, we will massively blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field.
statements.context: Truth Social post after Israel struck South Pars without notifying US, per Trump. CNN sources say coordination occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN and Jerusalem Post report Israel coordinated with US on the South Pars strike

statements.text: The $200 billion is a small price to pay to make sure we stay tippy top. I thought oil prices would be worse, much worse actually. We are not putting troops anywhere. It will be over pretty soon.
statements.context: Afternoon press conference remarks. Downplaying economic pain while requesting largest war supplemental since Iraq/Afghanistan.
statements.factCheckNote: Gas prices up 20%+, oil hit $119 intraday. 'Not putting troops anywhere' contradicts 2,200 Marines deployment. No timeline given for 'pretty soon.'

statements.text: Israel 'out of anger for what has taken place in the Middle East, has violently lashed out' at South Pars Gas Field. US 'knew nothing about this particular attack.'
statements.context: Truth Social post
statements.factCheckNote: Sources contradicted claim US didn't know in advance

statements.text: Threatened to 'massively blow up the entirety' of South Pars if Iran attacks Qatar again. Said NO US ground troops. Invoked Pearl Harbor during Japan PM meeting.
statements.context: White House remarks with Japan PM Takaichi
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast remarks

statements.text: When asked by a Japanese reporter why allies weren't warned about the Iran strikes, Trump quipped: 'Who knows better about surprise than Japan? Why didn't you tell me about Pearl Harbor?' He added the surprise element meant the US 'knocked out 50% of what we anticipated' in the first two days.
statements.context: Oval Office meeting with Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi; defending decision to launch strikes without notifying allies
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple outlets confirmed the remark drew gasps. CNN, CNBC, ABC News all confirmed.

statements.text: On US ground troops in Iran: 'No. I'm not putting troops anywhere.' Also stated: 'We're asking for a lot of reasons, beyond even what we're talking about in Iran, when this is a very volatile world,' regarding the $200B Pentagon request.
statements.context: Press availability on Day 21; ruling out ground invasion
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Newsweek and Fox News live updates.

statements.text: I told Netanyahu to hold off on hitting Iran energy sites. The United States knew nothing about this particular attack [on South Pars]. But if Iran keeps attacking Qatar, we will 'massively blow up the entirety of the South Pars Gas Field.'
statements.context: Truth Social post and public statements on the Israel-Iran South Pars dispute; Nowruz Day 21
statements.factCheckNote: Both Trump and Netanyahu confirmed the conversation

statements.text: NATO allies: COWARDS! Now that fight is Militarily WON, with very little danger for them, they complain about the high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don't want to help open the Strait of Hormuz, a simple military maneuver that is the single reason for the high oil prices. So easy for them to do, with so little risk. COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER! Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!
statements.context: Truth Social post on Day 21 (Nowruz/Eid al-Fitr) attacking NATO allies for refusing to help open the Strait of Hormuz despite six nations offering limited post-ceasefire assistance. Sharpest attack on NATO during the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Truth Social post confirmed by Bloomberg, CNN, Al Jazeera

statements.text: We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East.
statements.context: Truth Social post outlining five core war goals — destroying Iran's defense industrial base, missile capability, nuclear program, oil infrastructure, and leadership chain. Signal of potential operational end.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump claims objectives are met and war is 'militarily won' — but Hormuz remains effectively closed, Iran continues missile attacks on Israel, and Hegseth's own data shows uneven strike frequency.

statements.text: Now that fight is Militarily WON, with very little danger for them, they complain about the high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don't want to help open the Strait of Hormuz... COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER!
statements.context: Trump blasted NATO allies for refusing to commit forces to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Wrote: 'Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!'
statements.factCheckNote: Trump's 'militarily won' claim is disputed. Kallas confirmed EU states 'do not have an appetite' for military involvement; six allies issued a joint statement expressing readiness to 'help' without committing forces.

statements.text: Their leaders are all gone. The next set of leaders are all gone. And the next set of leaders are mostly gone... We want to talk to them and there's nobody to talk to.
statements.context: Speech before Naval Academy midshipmen at Annapolis. Trump said Iran's leadership had been so decimated there was no one left to negotiate with.
statements.factCheckNote: Many senior Iranian officials have been killed, but Mojtaba Khamenei released a Nowruz message on March 20 and Pezeshkian released statements the same day, indicating functional leadership still exists.

statements.text: We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East with respect to the Terrorist Regime of Iran. I think we've won.
statements.context: Trump posted on Truth Social that the US was 'getting very close to meeting objectives' and considering winding down operations. On the South Lawn, he told reporters 'I think we've won' and 'I don't want to do a cease-fire. You don't do a cease-fire when you're literally obliterating the other side.' Contradicted by simultaneous deployment of 2,500 additional Marines and Pentagon ground force planning.
statements.venue: Truth Social + South Lawn press gaggle
statements.factCheckNote: Contradicted by simultaneous deployment of 2,200–2,500 Marines from the 11th MEU aboard USS Boxer. Iran dismissed as 'psychological operations to control the markets.' IDF stated 3+ more weeks of operations needed.

statements.text: Nobody left to talk to [in Tehran]. There is nobody to call. As far as I am concerned, all the leaders of Iran are gone.
statements.context: Trump told reporters outside the White House that Iran's leadership has been eliminated and there is no one left for the US to negotiate with. He simultaneously denied sending ground troops to Iran: "If I were [sending troops], I certainly wouldn't tell you, but I'm not putting troops."
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's government continues to function. President Pezeshkian delivered a live Nowruz address on March 21. FM Araghchi appeared on NBC the same morning. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued statements March 20. Senior military officials continue to brief press.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz must be protected by other nations who use it. NATO is showing its COWARDICE by not supporting action to free up the world's most important shipping lane.
statements.context: Posted on Truth Social after UK authorized Diego Garcia for US Hormuz operations. Trump expressed frustration that NATO allies have not contributed militarily to re-opening the Strait of Hormuz, calling them cowards. Also said he would not put ground troops into the conflict.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 22 liveblog. 'Other nations have to protect Hormuz from Iran' per liveblog headline.

statements.text: If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!
statements.context: Truth Social post at 7:44 PM ET on March 21. The 48-hour deadline expires ~7:44 PM ET Monday March 23. Earlier the same day Trump had said he was 'considering winding down' military operations — this ultimatum reversed that signal. Trump also stated: 'You don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side.'
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by Fox News, Bloomberg, CNN, NBC. The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed since Day 3 (March 3). Iran has not agreed to open it.

statements.text: Why should we have a ceasefire when we are defeating Iran? Their leadership is gone, their navy and air force are dead, they have absolutely no defense. We are weeks ahead of schedule.
statements.context: Day 23 remarks rejecting ceasefire calls, shortly after issuing the 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran retains ballistic missile capacity (demonstrated by Arad/Dimona strikes Day 22). Iran's navy largely destroyed per CENTCOM — but claim of "no defense" disputed by Iranian missile activity.

statements.text: We are weeks ahead of schedule on Iran. Doing a great job. No deals. Iran must open the Strait of Hormuz, and open it now. The United States will not be stopped.
statements.context: Trump press remarks Day 23, claiming progress in the war effort.
statements.factCheckNote: CNN data analysis found pace of operations has 'ebbed and flowed' rather than consistently accelerating as claimed. Strike frequency claims previously overstated by Hegseth.

statements.text: If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!
statements.context: 48-hour ultimatum to Iran issued on Truth Social at 7:44 PM ET. Iran responded with full defiance, threatening to 'completely close' Hormuz and attack Gulf energy infrastructure if power plants are struck.
statements.factCheckNote: Ultimatum expires Monday March 23 ~7:44 PM ET. Iran has shown zero compliance signal. Multiple allies (UK PM Starmer, Germany's Merz) have urged restraint.

statements.text: I have met my own goals for this war… and weeks ahead of schedule! Their leadership is gone, their navy and air force are dead, they have absolutely no defense, and they want to make a deal.
statements.context: Claimed on Truth Social that US war objectives against Iran have been met ahead of schedule, contradicted by ongoing missile attacks on Israel and Pentagon's $200B supplemental request to Congress. Iran fired 8 missile salvos at Israel the same day.
statements.factCheckNote: Contradicted by Iran's continuing 8-salvo missile campaign on Israel, Hegseth stating 'no timeframe' for war, and Pentagon's $200B supplemental request. Intel community assessment leaked to WaPo says large-scale war 'unlikely' to oust Iran's regime.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Trump changing messages analysis

statements.text: We can talk, but I don't want a ceasefire. Iran's military has been dismantled. Halting operations now would be premature.
statements.context: Response to Pope Leo XIV's Sunday Angelus ceasefire appeal. Issued via White House spokesperson.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by National Catholic Register and Zenit news agency reporting on Vatican-US exchange.

statements.text: I am leaning toward traveling to Israel in April to receive the Israel Prize at their Independence Day ceremony. We will see.
statements.context: Informal remarks to reporters. Independence Day ceremony is April 22. Trip would be Trump's first to Israel since war began.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel liveblog March 22. Described as informal remark; no formal announcement made.

statements.text: Iran defied my 48-hour ultimatum. Their power plants are next. I don't want a ceasefire — we're crushing them. Their military is dismantled. We're weeks ahead of schedule.
statements.context: Day 24 morning statement reaffirming Hormuz stance and rejecting ceasefire appeals. Deadline expires tonight at approximately 7:44 PM ET.
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of being "weeks ahead of schedule" disputed by CNN analysis; Iran continues launching 8+ missile salvos/day with 150-180 Israelis wounded on Day 23. "Military dismantled" claim contradicted by ongoing strikes.

statements.text: I AM PLEASED TO REPORT THAT THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AND THE COUNTRY OF IRAN, HAVE HAD, OVER THE LAST TWO DAYS, VERY GOOD AND PRODUCTIVE CONVERSATIONS REGARDING A COMPLETE AND TOTAL RESOLUTION OF OUR HOSTILITIES IN THE MIDDLE EAST. Based on the tenor and tone of these in depth, detailed and constructive conversations, which will continue throughout the week, I have instructed the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period, subject to the success of the ongoing meetings and discussions.
statements.context: Truth Social post before the 48-hour Hormuz deadline expired. Trump also told CNBC: 'this is regime change,' saying many Iranian leaders have been killed and replaced. Iran's government immediately denied any negotiations had occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's FM Araghchi categorically denied any negotiations: 'No direct or indirect contact' with the US. Fars News confirmed Iranian denial. Trump's claim of 'productive conversations' contradicted by Iran's foreign ministry.
statements.source: NPR — Iran defiant as Trump delays striking power plants

statements.text: We're giving it a five-day window and we'll see how it goes. If it goes well, we'll wrap it up. Otherwise, we'll keep bombing. Iran agreed to no nukes, no enrichment — they'll also be surrendering their enriched uranium stockpile. Israel will be very happy. The Strait of Hormuz will be managed by mutual agreement of both sides. We've been speaking to Iran's most respected leader.
statements.context: Trump revealed claimed deal terms to reporters at West Palm Beach airport after announcing 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes.
statements.venue: West Palm Beach International Airport, FL
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Foreign Ministry flatly denied any negotiations or agreements. Trump's claims of 'no nukes, no enrichment' deal cannot be independently confirmed. Iran state media called the claims disinformation designed to lower oil prices.
statements.source: Times of Israel / Axios / Washington Post

statements.text: I am pleased to report that the United States of America, and the country of Iran, have had, over the last two days, very good and productive conversations regarding a complete and total resolution of our hostilities in the Middle East. I have instructed the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period.
statements.context: Trump Truth Social post announcing 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes. Came hours before his own 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum was set to expire. Iran immediately denied any talks occurred.
statements.factCheckNote: Trump claims "productive conversations" with Iran — Iran categorically denied any negotiations. The US has not identified the Iranian interlocutor. Third-party intermediaries (Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey) are believed to be relaying messages.

statements.text: They want to make a deal. They have agreed they will not have nuclear weapons. They agreed they will not have enriched uranium. They have to give up their enriched uranium. Hormuz will be jointly managed. At the end of this period I think it could very well end up being a very good deal.
statements.context: Trump press conference remarks outlining claimed Iran deal terms. Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff named as US envoys in talks. Iran denied all these claimed terms and any negotiations.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denied agreeing to any of the stated terms. No independent confirmation of any Iranian nuclear concession. Deal terms remain unverified.

statements.text: We are very intent on making a deal. They will never have a nuclear weapon — that is number one, two and three. They must reopen Hormuz and surrender their enriched uranium stockpiles. We have been having very good and productive conversations.
statements.context: Statement to reporters and on Truth Social regarding US-Iran negotiations. Trump confirmed Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff were leading talks. Announced a 5-day pause on strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure pending outcome of talks.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran publicly denied any negotiations. Third-party intermediaries (Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey) believed to be relaying messages. Iran International separately reported Araghchi privately signaled readiness.

statements.text: Pete, I think you were the first one to speak up and you said let's do it because you can't let them have a nuclear weapon. We're in negotiations right now. And the other side, I can tell you they'd like to make a deal.
statements.context: Trump credited Hegseth as the primary advocate for the Iran war, deflecting responsibility as the conflict grows politically unpopular. Trump also referenced a mystery Iran "present" worth a "tremendous amount of money" that was "oil and gas related."
statements.factCheckNote: Iran publicly denied any negotiations. The "deal" terms Trump described (no enrichment, surrender uranium) were not confirmed by Iran.

statements.text: WE HAVE WON THIS WAR. Iran has agreed to NEVER acquire nuclear weapons. The Strait of Hormuz is going to open. We have received a very significant prize from Iran. Very good and productive conversations are happening right now — between Kushner, Witkoff, Rubio, Vance, and Iranian officials.
statements.context: Truth Social post (all-caps as originally posted); later elaborated at Tennessee roundtable
statements.factCheckNote: Iran publicly denied direct negotiations. Indirect contacts confirmed via Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt. "Significant prize" unspecified. Iran International reported Araghchi privately signaled deal readiness via back-channel.

statements.text: I am pausing strikes on Iranian power plants for 5 days to give diplomacy a chance. Very significant things are happening. The Iranians are showing great flexibility. We are being very tough, but fair.
statements.context: Announcement of 5-day pause on power plant strikes — pause expires approximately March 28
statements.factCheckNote: Strike pause confirmed by Pentagon briefings. Iranian response to the deadline remains unclear as of this report.

statements.text: The Iranian regime is the single greatest state sponsor of terrorism in the world and must be confronted.
statements.context: As a senator on the Foreign Relations Committee, Rubio delivered a floor speech advocating for stronger measures against Iran, aligning with his long-standing hawkish position.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. State Department does designate Iran as a state sponsor of terrorism, the characterization of 'single greatest' is a subjective ranking. Iran's role is significant but other state actors also contribute to global instability.

statements.text: Any deal with Iran that does not address ballistic missiles and regional aggression is worse than no deal at all.
statements.context: Rubio criticized ongoing European diplomatic efforts to revive nuclear talks with Iran, arguing that a narrow focus on nuclear enrichment ignored larger security threats.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a policy opinion rather than a factual claim. Nonproliferation experts are divided on whether linking missile programs to nuclear talks helps or hinders diplomatic progress.

statements.text: Iran's proxies have American blood on their hands. Hezbollah, Hamas, the Houthis — all roads lead back to Tehran.
statements.context: Following attacks on U.S. military positions in Syria and Iraq by Iran-backed militias, Rubio called for direct retaliation against Iranian assets.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran provides support to these groups, each operates with varying degrees of autonomy. Characterizing all proxy actions as directly ordered by Tehran oversimplifies complex regional dynamics. Responsibility for specific attacks requires individual attribution.

statements.text: As Secretary of State, I will ensure that America's adversaries understand that the era of appeasement is over.
statements.context: In his first major address after confirmation as Secretary of State, Rubio signaled a dramatic shift in diplomatic posture, explicitly naming Iran, China, and Russia.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of previous administrations' policies as 'appeasement' is a political framing. The JCPOA and related diplomatic efforts are viewed by their architects as strategic engagement, not appeasement.

statements.text: We have exhausted every diplomatic avenue with Iran. They have chosen escalation at every turn.
statements.context: Rubio defended the administration's decision to end all back-channel communications with Iran, a move criticized by career diplomats as premature.
statements.factCheckDetail: Multiple diplomatic channels remained available but were not pursued. European allies and Oman had offered to mediate, and Iran's foreign ministry had signaled openness to preliminary discussions. The claim of exhausting every avenue is contradicted by these documented opportunities.

statements.text: Israel has an inherent right to self-defense. We will not second-guess our ally when it faces an existential threat.
statements.context: Responding to IAEA reports of Iran reaching near-weapons-grade enrichment levels, Rubio preemptively endorsed potential Israeli military action.
statements.factCheckDetail: Israel does have a right to self-defense under Article 51 of the UN Charter. However, legal scholars debate whether preemptive strikes against nuclear facilities qualify under this provision without an imminent armed attack.

statements.text: This administration provided intelligence support to Israel as any ally would. We did not plan or direct these operations.
statements.context: After Israeli strikes began, Rubio clarified the U.S. role, attempting to distinguish between intelligence sharing and direct operational involvement amid international criticism.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. may not have directly planned strike packages, extensive intelligence sharing, refueling support for Israeli aircraft, and satellite data constitute significant operational enablement that goes beyond routine allied cooperation.

statements.text: We are urging all parties to exercise restraint while maintaining our ironclad commitment to Israel's security.
statements.context: Rubio's statement attempted to thread the needle between calls for de-escalation from the international community and continued support for Israeli operations, drawing criticism for being contradictory.
statements.factCheckDetail: Critics pointed out the contradiction between calling for restraint while simultaneously authorizing emergency weapons shipments to Israel. The U.S. also vetoed a ceasefire resolution at the same Security Council session.

statements.text: Ordered all embassies to urge allies to pressure Iran 'at highest level' by March 20. Iran 'more sensitive to collective action.'
statements.context: Diplomatic cable
statements.factCheckNote: ABC News obtained cable

statements.text: We assess that the Iranian regime is more sensitive to collective action than unilateral action... We must act while international attention is focused now.
statements.context: Cable to all US diplomatic posts with action deadline of March 20, directing ambassadors to push host governments to designate IRGC and Hezbollah as terrorist organizations. Part of broader diplomatic pressure campaign.
statements.venue: State Department Cable to all diplomatic posts
statements.factCheckNote: Internal diplomatic communications — factual assessment not a public claim.

statements.text: I will travel to Cernay-la-Ville, France this Friday for the G7 Foreign Ministers meeting to coordinate with our allies on the path forward in the Middle East. The diplomatic track is open and active. We are pursuing every avenue to resolve this conflict consistent with the security interests of the United States and Israel.
statements.context: State Department announcement of G7 travel; also participating in Iran back-channel talks per Trump
statements.factCheckNote: G7 foreign ministers meeting confirmed. Rubio participation in Iran talks per Trump statement at Tennessee roundtable.

statements.text: Our military is the most powerful force in history. Any adversary who tests us will regret it.
statements.context: In his first Pentagon press briefing, Hegseth emphasized military readiness and deterrence, setting a combative tone for his tenure.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. military is widely considered the world's most capable, the characterization of 'most powerful in history' is a subjective claim. Readiness assessments from the GAO have identified specific capability gaps.

statements.text: Iran's air defense systems are outdated Soviet-era relics. They would not survive a serious engagement.
statements.context: In an interview, Hegseth appeared to dismiss Iranian military capabilities, a statement that drew pushback from military analysts who noted Iran's investment in indigenous defense systems.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran has significantly modernized its air defense network, including the domestically produced Bavar-373 system and Russian-supplied S-300 batteries. Military analysts assess these systems as capable of posing meaningful threats to attacking aircraft.

statements.text: We have positioned our assets to ensure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Commerce will not be disrupted.
statements.context: Hegseth announced the deployment of an additional carrier strike group to the Persian Gulf amid rising tensions and Iranian naval exercises near the Strait.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the U.S. deployed additional naval assets, Iran's extensive arsenal of anti-ship missiles, fast attack boats, and mine-laying capabilities mean a guarantee of uninterrupted commerce is not militarily supportable. Shipping insurance rates rose 400% in this period.

statements.text: Our troops in the region are positioned defensively. We are not seeking conflict but we are prepared for any scenario.
statements.context: Hegseth sought to reassure Congress after reports indicated U.S. force levels in the Middle East had reached their highest point since 2003.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of deployments as purely 'defensive' was questioned when leaked operational plans showed pre-positioned strike assets and forward-deployed special operations units. The buildup exceeded what military experts considered necessary for a purely defensive posture.

statements.text: We provided no direct military support for Israeli operations. U.S. forces are not engaged in combat operations against Iran.
statements.context: Hours before Israeli strikes commenced, Hegseth denied U.S. combat involvement, though reports later indicated U.S. aerial refueling tankers supported Israeli strike aircraft.
statements.factCheckDetail: While U.S. forces may not have directly fired weapons, aerial refueling of Israeli strike aircraft, intelligence sharing, and AWACS support constitute meaningful military support for combat operations, even if not classified as direct combat engagement.

statements.text: Iranian ballistic missile capabilities have been significantly degraded. The threat to our regional partners is diminished.
statements.context: Hegseth provided a battle damage assessment claiming Israeli and allied operations had destroyed a substantial portion of Iran's missile infrastructure.
statements.factCheckDetail: Independent verification of battle damage claims has not been possible due to access restrictions. Iran maintains dispersed and hardened missile facilities, and intelligence agencies have historically struggled to assess underground facility damage from satellite imagery alone.

statements.text: Force protection of our 45,000 personnel in the region remains my top priority. We are ready for any contingency.
statements.context: As Iranian-aligned militia attacks on U.S. bases in Iraq and Syria intensified, Hegseth confirmed troop numbers that had not been officially disclosed, drawing criticism for the operational security lapse.
statements.factCheckDetail: The troop figure, while not previously confirmed, was broadly consistent with independent estimates. The statement of prioritizing force protection is consistent with official DoD policy.

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury is on track as we enter the second week. We have spared no expense or capability on air defense, but we can't stop everything.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on Day 10. Signaled the conflict could last 4-8 weeks. Dismissed criticisms of the war effort while acknowledging limits of air defense systems.
statements.factCheckDetail: The acknowledgment of air defense limitations is consistent with reports of successful Iranian strikes on Gulf state infrastructure. The 4-8 week timeline represents first official duration estimate.

statements.text: Mojtaba Khamenei is likely disfigured. Every ballistic missile company in Iran has been destroyed. We have hit over 15,000 targets across the theater.
statements.context: Day 14 Pentagon briefing. Hegseth claimed Khamenei was 'likely disfigured' from initial strikes. Stated every ballistic missile production facility destroyed. 15,000+ targets figure is highest official count yet. Also confirmed IDF destroyed 250+ Iranian drones.
statements.factCheckNote: 15,000 targets figure significantly exceeds Trump's 6,000 claim — discrepancy may reflect different counting methodologies (individual aimpoints vs. target complexes). Khamenei's condition unverified independently.

statements.text: This is the highest volume of strikes we've put over Iran's skies. Over 15,000 enemy targets struck. Iran's missile capacity is down 90%, drone capacity down 95%. Ending Iran's nuclear program remains our core mission.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing. Confirmed 2,200 Marines deployed. Would not rule out ground forces to secure enriched uranium.
statements.factCheckNote: Strike volume confirmed. Specific degradation percentages are US claims — independent verification limited.

statements.text: Iran's military is 'destroyed' and 'combat ineffective.' Entire ballistic missile production capacity has been 'eliminated.' Missile volume down 90%, drone volume down 95%. March 14 will see the highest volume of US strikes yet. Confirmed CENTCOM investigation into Minab school strike with outside investigating officer.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing, Day 14
statements.factCheckNote: Missile/drone reduction figures not independently verified. School strike investigation confirmed.

statements.text: New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is 'wounded and likely disfigured.' State Department has offered $10 million reward for information on Khamenei's location. US-Israeli strikes have hit 15,000+ targets across 200+ cities.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on supreme leader targeting and overall strike campaign scope
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's condition not independently confirmed. $10M reward confirmed by State Department.

statements.text: Iranian forces launched thousands of drones AND missiles at its neighbours.
statements.context: CENTCOM X post responding to Iranian FM Araghchi's claims. Hegseth/CENTCOM pushed back on Iranian narrative about the scope of Iranian attacks on regional neighbors.
statements.factCheckNote: CENTCOM statement confirmed. Iran has launched missiles at Gulf states, Iraq, and Israel throughout the conflict.

statements.text: We are launching the largest strike package yet on Iran today. It takes money to kill bad guys. That $200 billion number could move.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on March 19. Announced 7,000+ targets struck total, 64 Apache helicopters deployed against Iranian drones.
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News confirmed briefing details

statements.text: Operation Epic Fury is not an endless abyss or a forever war or quagmire. Over 120 Iranian naval vessels sunk. 11 submarines eliminated. Iran's surface fleet is no longer a factor. It takes money to kill bad guys.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing with Gen. Caine. Confirmed 5,000-lb penetrators, A-10s hunting boats in Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Naval destruction figures consistent with previous claims. 'Not a forever war' claim undercut by refusal to provide timeline.

statements.text: We will launch the largest strike package yet today. We've struck over 7,800 targets. All 11 submarines destroyed, 120+ warships sunk. It takes money to kill bad guys.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing on Day 20 operations and $200B war supplemental request
statements.factCheckNote: Pentagon briefing aired live

statements.text: $200B supplemental: 'That number could move. It takes money to kill bad guys.' NOT a 'forever war or quagmire.' No timeline for end. Over 7,000 targets struck.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing
statements.factCheckNote: Briefing broadcast

statements.text: Announced the US would launch its 'largest strike package yet' against Iran. Said US has struck more than 7,000 targets, destroyed all 11 Iranian submarines, sunk or damaged 120+ warships. Ballistic missile attacks on US forces down 90% since conflict began.
statements.context: Pentagon briefing alongside Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine; Day 21 press conference
statements.factCheckNote: CBS News, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel confirmed the briefing and figures.

statements.text: It takes money to kill bad guys.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing on March 19 when asked about the $200 billion supplemental war funding request — four times the originally floated figure. Hegseth also said the US had struck over 7,000 targets in Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement is rhetorical, not factual. The $200B request figure is confirmed by multiple sources.

statements.text: Iran is not deterred — they are determined. And so are we. The American military will continue to prosecute this campaign until Iran's ability to threaten its neighbors, fund terrorism, and pursue nuclear weapons is decisively degraded. We will not stop.
statements.context: Hegseth's public statement on Day 23 as Iran fired 8 missile salvos at Israel and Trump's power plant ultimatum deadline approached.
statements.factCheckNote: Public statement consistent with US posture throughout the conflict.

statements.text: We negotiate with bombs. You have a choice, as we loiter over the top of Tehran. The Iranian Air Force is destroyed. The Iranian Navy is sunk in the Persian Gulf. We have achieved an 86% reduction in Iranian ballistic missile launches since Day 1, and a 73% reduction in one-way attack drones.
statements.context: Pentagon press briefing alongside Gen. Dan Caine on Day 25 operational status
statements.factCheckNote: 86% reduction in missile launches and 73% in drone attacks are self-reported Pentagon figures; not independently verified by external analysts.
statements.source: The Hill / Business Today / Pentagon briefing

statements.text: Iran is the greatest threat to world peace. A nuclear Iran is an existential threat to the State of Israel and to all civilization.
statements.context: In an address to a joint session of Congress, Netanyahu framed the Iran nuclear issue as a global civilizational threat, seeking to build international support for action.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran's nuclear program poses legitimate proliferation concerns, characterizing it as the 'greatest threat to world peace' and 'all civilization' is considered hyperbolic by most international security analysts, who cite multiple concurrent global threats.

statements.text: Israel will not allow Iran to acquire nuclear weapons. We reserve the right to act alone if necessary.
statements.context: At the UN General Assembly, Netanyahu reiterated Israel's red line on Iranian nuclear capability, signaling willingness for unilateral military action.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents Israel's longstanding stated policy position. Israel's Begin Doctrine, established after the 1981 Osirak reactor strike, holds that Israel will prevent hostile states from acquiring nuclear weapons.

statements.text: Iran's attack on Israeli soil was an unprecedented act of aggression. We will respond at a time and manner of our choosing.
statements.context: Following Iran's direct missile and drone attack on Israel in April 2024, Netanyahu addressed the nation and pledged a calculated response.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran did conduct a direct military strike on Israeli territory in April 2024, the first such direct attack. The characterization of the event as unprecedented and aggressive is factually supported.

statements.text: We welcome the return of President Trump. Israel has never had a greater friend in the White House.
statements.context: After the 2024 U.S. election, Netanyahu praised Trump's victory and anticipated closer coordination on Iran policy.
statements.factCheckDetail: The characterization of Trump as Israel's greatest White House friend is subjective. While Trump recognized Jerusalem and the Golan Heights, other presidents have provided significant support including military aid packages and diplomatic backing.

statements.text: Our intelligence indicates Iran is months away from a nuclear weapon. The world cannot afford to wait.
statements.context: Netanyahu presented intelligence briefings to allied leaders claiming Iran had crossed critical enrichment thresholds, building the case for preventive action.
statements.factCheckDetail: Israeli intelligence on Iran's nuclear timeline has historically been contested. Previous claims of imminent Iranian nuclear capability dating back to the 1990s did not materialize. Independent IAEA assessments noted increased enrichment but did not confirm weaponization timelines.

statements.text: Two years after October 7th, Israel is stronger and more determined than ever. We will eliminate every threat to our existence.
statements.context: On the second anniversary of the October 7 attacks, Netanyahu connected the ongoing Iran threat to the broader security environment, using the memorial to justify expanded military operations.
statements.factCheckDetail: Claims of increased strength are difficult to independently verify. While Israel's military capabilities remain formidable, the prolonged multi-front conflicts have strained resources and personnel according to IDF reserve force assessments.

statements.text: We will not live under the shadow of a nuclear threat. Tonight, Israel acts to ensure its survival for generations.
statements.context: Netanyahu's address to the nation as Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities commenced, framing the operation as an existential necessity.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the nuclear threat concern has some basis, the characterization of the strikes as purely defensive is contested. Critics argue Israel possesses its own undeclared nuclear arsenal, creating a deterrence dynamic that the 'survival' framing does not acknowledge.

statements.text: Our operations have been precise and limited to military and nuclear targets. We have no quarrel with the Iranian people.
statements.context: Amid reports of civilian casualties near targeted facilities, Netanyahu defended the operation's targeting methodology and attempted to separate the conflict from the Iranian civilian population.
statements.factCheckDetail: While strikes may have targeted military and nuclear facilities, independent reports from humanitarian organizations documented civilian casualties in surrounding areas. Infrastructure damage also affected civilian populations through power and water disruptions.

statements.text: We are open to a ceasefire if Iran permanently dismantles its nuclear program and its proxy network. These are reasonable terms.
statements.context: Under growing international pressure, Netanyahu set conditions for a ceasefire that were widely seen as non-starters designed to appear diplomatic while ensuring continued operations.
statements.factCheckDetail: Demanding permanent dismantlement of both nuclear programs and proxy networks as preconditions for ceasefire is considered diplomatically unrealistic. Analysts note these conditions exceed what was achieved through years of JCPOA negotiations and are designed to be unacceptable.

statements.text: Israel will continue wide-scale strikes on Iran's military and energy infrastructure until the threat is eliminated.
statements.context: IDF briefing.

statements.text: We are breaking their bones. The moment of truth is close. We are trying to free Iran. There will be many surprises in the next phase.
statements.context: Netanyahu addressed the Iranian people directly, vowing 'many surprises.' Diverging from Trump's 'war ending soon' signals — Netanyahu wants to continue operations while Trump pushes de-escalation. Bloomberg reports widening gap between US and Israeli endgames.
statements.factCheckDetail: Netanyahu frames strikes as liberation of Iranians, but 1,380+ civilians killed undermines this framing. IDF has struck 170+ munitions in Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz including IRGC Quds Force HQ.

statements.text: If Lebanon does not rein in Hezbollah, we will strike national infrastructure. We have the capability and the will to take territory.
statements.context: Threat conveyed through US and Western channels to Lebanon's government. Defense Minister Katz made similar statements. Follows largest Hezbollah barrage of the war.

statements.text: We are crushing Iran and Hezbollah. Mojtaba Khamenei is an IRGC puppet who cannot show his face in public. To the Iranian people: the coming days will bring an opportunity to free yourselves. Be ready.
statements.context: First press conference since war began. Netanyahu vowed 'many surprises' for the next phase.
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live; quotes confirmed by Times of Israel, PBS, Haaretz

statements.text: Israel's defense chief has declared we will hold Lebanese territory until Hezbollah fully disarms. There will be no withdrawal without security guarantees.
statements.context: Day 14 statement via defense establishment channels. Israel defense chief announced intention to hold occupied Lebanese land until Hezbollah disarms — echoing 2006 war posture but with expanded territorial aims.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz and CNN reporting on Israel defense chief statements.

statements.text: This IRGC puppet Mojtaba Khamenei can't even show his face in public. This is not an endless war — it will usher in an era of peace. There will be many surprises for the next phase.
statements.context: First press conference of the war and Fox News Hannity interview. Scoffed at new Supreme Leader's inability to appear publicly.
statements.factCheckNote: Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement was read by state TV anchor, not delivered in person.

statements.text: Our strikes are creating optimal conditions for toppling the regime, but I cannot guarantee regime change without an internal uprising. I wouldn't take out a life insurance policy on any of their leaders.
statements.context: Press conference addressing regime change goals and threats against Iranian leadership
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Bloomberg; press conference footage available

statements.text: [Video posted showing himself alive and well]
statements.context: Netanyahu posted a video to counter Iranian state media rumors that he had been killed. The video showed him in an undisclosed location, appearing unharmed.
statements.factCheckNote: Video confirmed authentic. Posted in direct response to Iranian propaganda claiming Netanyahu was dead.

statements.text: I am dying... for a coffee.
statements.context: Netanyahu posted video from Tel Aviv cafe mocking conspiracy theories about his death. Spread all five fingers visibly to counter AI deepfake '6 finger' claims.
statements.factCheckNote: Reuters confirmed Netanyahu's presence via cafe surveillance cameras. Snopes debunked the 6-finger deepfake claim.

statements.text: We will eradicate Hezbollah from Lebanon. The IDF's ground operation will continue until the threat to our northern border is eliminated.
statements.context: Statement following IDF ground operations into southern Lebanon. 91st Division took Khiam. 450,000 reservists called up.
statements.factCheckNote: IDF confirmed ground operations in southern Lebanon. However, 'eradicate' language is aspirational given Hezbollah's deep roots in Lebanese society.

statements.text: Go out and celebrate. We're watching from above.
statements.context: Video message urging Iranians to celebrate Chaharshanbe Suri fire festival as anti-regime protest
statements.factCheckNote: Video confirmed by PM's office

statements.text: We are watching you from above. The brave people of Iran deserve a year of freedom.
statements.context: Nowruz greeting video from Kirya HQ, referencing Larijani/Soleimani kills
statements.factCheckNote: JPost confirmed

statements.text: The strike on South Pars sends a clear message: there is no safe haven for Iran's military infrastructure. We will continue striking until Iran's ability to threaten Israel is fully degraded.
statements.context: Statement after IAF strikes on South Pars gas field and Bandar Anzali naval base
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Times of Israel

statements.text: Iran can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles. Israel needs a ground component for regime change. I see this war ending a lot faster than people think. Iran is no longer the same Iran.
statements.context: Evening press conference, March 19. First explicit call for ground offensive and regime change.
statements.factCheckNote: No evidence provided for uranium enrichment claim. IAEA chief Grossi: 'the material will still be there.' Ground offensive claim represents major policy shift.

statements.text: Iran can no longer enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles. Regime change requires 'ground component.' On South Pars: 'Israel acted alone. President Trump asked us to hold off, and we're holding off.'
statements.context: 2nd press conference since war
statements.factCheckNote: Nuclear/missile claims unverified independently

statements.text: Mocked Mojtaba: 'I am not sure who is ruling Iran. Mojtaba, the replacement Ayatollah, has yet to appear.' Denied dragging US into war.
statements.context: Press conference
statements.factCheckNote: Broadcast live

statements.text: Confirmed Israel struck Tehran again on Nowruz/Persian New Year. On IRGC spokesman Naeini's claim Iran was still building missiles: 'He was wrong. Dead wrong.' Netanyahu stated Israel will hold off future attacks on energy sites at Trump's request: 'President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off.'
statements.context: Statement on Day 21 strikes; Naeini killed shortly after his claim Iran still building missiles
statements.factCheckNote: Times of Israel liveblog March 20 confirmed; Fox News and NPR confirmed.

statements.text: President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding out. Israel acted alone in hitting the South Pars gas field.
statements.context: Statement on Nowruz; confirming Trump-Netanyahu rift on energy infrastructure targeting
statements.factCheckNote: Directly confirmed by Netanyahu; consistent with Trump statement

statements.text: Fact number one, Israel acted alone against the Asaluyeh gas compound. Fact number two, President Trump asked us to hold off on future attacks, and we're holding off.
statements.context: First English-language press conference since the war began. Netanyahu admitted Israel unilaterally struck South Pars (Asaluyeh), contradicting earlier reports that the US had advance knowledge. Confirmed agreement with Trump to pause energy infrastructure strikes unless Iran attacks Qatar.
statements.factCheckNote: Netanyahu's claim that Israel 'acted alone' contradicts reporting by anonymous Israeli officials who initially said the US had advance knowledge of the South Pars strike.

statements.text: You can't make a revolution from the air... there must be a ground component — I won't share with you all the options.
statements.context: At the same press conference, signaling that Israeli planning includes a ground component, raising concerns about a possible land invasion of Iran or Lebanon escalation.
statements.factCheckNote: Forward-looking strategic statement; not a factual claim.

statements.text: This is a very difficult evening in the campaign for our future. We are determined to continue to strike our enemies on all fronts.
statements.context: Statement issued after Iranian ballistic missiles struck Arad (74 injured, air defense failure) and Dimona (47 injured, adjacent to nuclear research center) on Day 22 evening.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by France 24, CBS News live updates, multiple Israeli media.

statements.text: If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it. They fired on Jerusalem right next to the holy sites of the three monotheistic faiths. They have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. It's time to see the leaders of the rest of the countries join up.
statements.context: Statement at Arad — site of the Day 22 Iranian IRBM direct hit — urging Europe to join military coalition against Iran. Cited Iran's Diego Garcia strike as proof Berlin, Paris, and Rome are within reach.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Diego Garcia IRBM strike (intercepted, ~4,000km) confirmed by WSJ/CNBC. The ~4,000km range is factually within range of European capitals.

statements.text: If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have given it. It's time for world leaders to join us, and I'm seeing movement from some in that direction. What more proof do you need that this regime that threatens the entire world has to be stopped?
statements.context: Netanyahu spoke at the missile strike site in Arad, where Iranian ballistic missiles struck residential buildings overnight wounding 115+ people. He cited three events: Arad attack, missiles fired toward Jerusalem near holy sites, and Diego Garcia ICBM attempt.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel. Netanyahu visited Arad on March 22.

statements.text: President Trump's call to have the international community confront this fanatic terrorist regime of zealots is a call not only for the security of America and the security of Israel; it's for the security of the entire world. It's time for them to act.
statements.context: Statement at Arad missile strike site, elaborating on coalition-building appeal after Trump's call for global participation in the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Tribune India/ANI and Jerusalem Post reporting from Arad visit.

statements.text: Whatever we do, we do together, and as far as possible, in confidence. President Trump sees an opportunity to achieve the war's objectives through an agreement with Iran. Israel will be very happy with the terms Trump has described. Our attacks on Iran and Lebanon continue regardless of diplomatic signals.
statements.context: Netanyahu backed US strikes on Iran's power grid if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and supported Trump's claim of productive talks while vowing Israeli operations continue at full force.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera video newsfeed and Haaretz live updates. IDF simultaneously confirmed operations continue 'at full force.'
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Netanyahu backs US strikes on Iran

statements.text: Israel will protect our vital interests in every situation. Iran's attack on Diego Garcia proves they have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. They are putting everyone in their sights — it is time for the leaders of the rest of the countries to join up.
statements.context: Netanyahu first remarks since Trump announced Iran talks. Called on world leaders to join coalition, warning Europe of Iranian IRBM range revealed by Diego Garcia attack (March 21). Separately spoke with VP JD Vance about deal components.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran confirmed the Diego Garcia attack. Missile range 4,000km verified by UK government. Call with Vance confirmed by Israeli officials.

statements.text: I believe it is possible to leverage the mighty achievements obtained by the US and Israeli military to realize the goals of the war in an agreement that will safeguard our vital interests. Israel acted alone in striking the Asaluyeh gas complex. We are complying with President Trump's request to pause further such strikes.
statements.context: Statement after Trump's announcement of US-Iran talks. Netanyahu confirmed Israel was acting in coordination with Trump, and confirmed Israel alone struck South Pars/Asaluyeh gas compound.
statements.factCheckNote: Israeli strike on South Pars/Asaluyeh independently confirmed. Compliance with Trump request confirmed by multiple Israeli officials.

statements.text: We are continuing to strike in both Iran and Lebanon. We are smashing the missile program and the nuclear program, and we continue to deal severe blows to Hezbollah… there is more to come. I believe it is possible to leverage the mighty achievements obtained by the US and Israeli military to realize the goals of the war in an agreement.
statements.context: Netanyahu video statement on Day 25, reaffirming the war will continue. Despite Trump's 5-day pause on energy infrastructure strikes, Israel continued striking Iran. Netanyahu is also reportedly frustrated that Mossad's promised Iranian popular uprising has not materialized.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera Day 25 and Haaretz. Nuclear program strikes confirmed by IDF.

statements.text: The Zionist regime is a cancerous tumor that the resistance front will ultimately uproot. This is a divine promise.
statements.context: In an address marking the anniversary of the Islamic Revolution, Khamenei used extreme rhetoric about Israel while framing Iranian regional strategy in religious terms.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is ideological and religious rhetoric rather than a factual claim. It represents the Supreme Leader's longstanding stated position on Israel, though the practical implications of such statements are debated among analysts.

statements.text: America's maximum pressure failed before and it will fail again. Iran's economy has proven resilient against sanctions.
statements.context: Responding to renewed U.S. sanctions rhetoric, Khamenei pointed to Iran's adaptation to previous sanctions as evidence of resilience.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran's economy did not collapse under sanctions, it suffered significant contraction, currency devaluation, and reduced oil exports. 'Resilient' masks genuine economic hardship experienced by ordinary Iranians, including inflation exceeding 40%.

statements.text: Our response to Israeli aggression demonstrated that Iran will defend itself. The era of unanswered attacks is over.
statements.context: After Iran's direct missile and drone attack on Israel, Khamenei framed the operation as a new deterrence paradigm, claiming Iran had established a precedent of direct retaliation.
statements.factCheckDetail: Iran's April 2024 attack on Israel did represent a significant escalation and departure from Iran's previous policy of operating primarily through proxies. The characterization of establishing a new norm of direct retaliation is factually supported by the event.

statements.text: Trump's return changes nothing. Iran does not negotiate under threats. Our nuclear program is peaceful and non-negotiable.
statements.context: On the day of Trump's inauguration, Khamenei issued a statement rejecting any preconditions for negotiations and reaffirming Iran's stated position on its nuclear program.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim that Iran's nuclear program is entirely peaceful is contradicted by IAEA reports documenting enrichment to 60% purity, far beyond civilian energy needs. Iran has also restricted IAEA inspector access, undermining transparency claims.

statements.text: We possess the technical capability to defend our nation by any means necessary. Our scientists' achievements cannot be bombed away.
statements.context: In a statement widely interpreted as a veiled reference to nuclear weapons capability, Khamenei used ambiguous language that stopped short of declaring a weapons program while implying deterrent capacity.
statements.factCheckDetail: The deliberately ambiguous language makes fact-checking difficult. Intelligence agencies disagree on whether Iran has achieved weapons-breakout capability. The claim that knowledge cannot be destroyed by bombing is generally true regarding scientific expertise, though infrastructure can be damaged.

statements.text: The Zionist enemy has made a grave miscalculation. They have opened the gates of hell upon themselves.
statements.context: Hours after Israeli strikes began on Iranian nuclear facilities, Khamenei issued a brief statement promising severe retaliation.
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a threat and rhetorical statement rather than a verifiable factual claim. The characterization of the strikes as a 'miscalculation' represents Iran's strategic assessment but cannot be independently verified.

statements.text: The resistance axis stands united. Every attack on Iran strengthens our allies' resolve from Beirut to Baghdad to Sana'a.
statements.context: As Iranian-aligned groups across the region launched retaliatory attacks, Khamenei framed the conflict as a validation of Iran's alliance network.
statements.venue: Address to Supreme National Security Council
statements.factCheckDetail: While proxy groups did escalate attacks, reports indicated coordination challenges and some groups acting independently. Hezbollah's capacity had been significantly degraded by prior Israeli operations in Lebanon, undermining the claim of unified strength.

statements.text: We will never surrender. Iran has endured for thousands of years and will outlast this aggression as it has outlasted all others.
statements.context: In a national address as the conflict intensified, Khamenei invoked Persian historical resilience to rally domestic support amid reports of significant infrastructure damage.
statements.factCheckDetail: The historical framing is largely rhetorical. Iran does have a long civilizational history, but the analogy to current geopolitical conflict is more motivational than analytical. The practical outcome of the current conflict remains undetermined.

statements.text: I believe in constructive engagement with the world. Iran's security is best served through diplomacy, not isolation.
statements.context: During his presidential campaign, Pezeshkian distinguished himself from hardline candidates by advocating for renewed diplomatic engagement with the West.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents Pezeshkian's stated campaign platform, which emphasized diplomatic engagement. His election was widely seen as a public mandate for moderation, though the Supreme Leader retains final authority on foreign policy.

statements.text: Iran is ready to return to the negotiating table without preconditions. We seek a deal that respects our sovereignty and rights.
statements.context: In his debut address at the UN General Assembly as president, Pezeshkian extended an olive branch to Western nations, signaling openness to new nuclear negotiations.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Pezeshkian expressed willingness to negotiate, the Supreme Leader's office subsequently clarified that certain conditions remained, including sanctions relief before substantive talks. The president's authority on nuclear policy is limited compared to the Supreme Leader.

statements.text: The Trump administration has not responded to our diplomatic signals. We cannot negotiate with people who only speak in threats.
statements.context: After months of unanswered diplomatic overtures, Pezeshkian expressed frustration with the lack of U.S. engagement, marking a shift in his initially optimistic tone.
