statements.factCheckDetail: Multiple diplomatic sources confirmed that Iranian back-channel communications were not reciprocated by the Trump administration. European mediators corroborated the lack of U.S. engagement with Iranian diplomatic proposals.

statements.text: Our nuclear program serves our energy needs and our scientific advancement. We have no interest in weapons of destruction.
statements.context: Responding to Israeli intelligence claims about Iran's nuclear progress, Pezeshkian maintained the official position that the program was entirely civilian.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Iran claims its nuclear program is peaceful, enrichment to 60% purity significantly exceeds civilian energy requirements (3-5%). IAEA reports have documented activities inconsistent with a purely civilian program, including restricted inspector access to certain facilities.

statements.text: I urge the international community to intervene before it is too late. There are those who want war, and we are not among them.
statements.context: As tensions escalated to critical levels, Pezeshkian made a direct appeal to the UN and European leaders, positioning Iran as seeking peace while Israel and the U.S. pushed toward conflict.
statements.factCheckDetail: While Pezeshkian's stated desire for peace may be genuine, Iran's continued enrichment activities, expansion of proxy operations, and the Supreme Leader's inflammatory rhetoric complicated the credibility of positioning Iran as solely peace-seeking.

statements.text: This unprovoked attack on our sovereign territory is an act of war. We call on all nations to condemn this aggression.
statements.context: Pezeshkian issued a formal statement condemning Israeli strikes, invoking international law and calling for a UN Security Council emergency session.
statements.factCheckDetail: While the strikes did target Iranian sovereign territory, the characterization of 'unprovoked' is contested. Israel cited Iran's nuclear advancement and support for proxy attacks as provocation. The legal classification of the strikes under international law is a matter of ongoing debate.

statements.text: Even now, I call for an immediate ceasefire and international mediation. War serves no one's interests, least of all the people.
statements.context: Breaking from the Supreme Leader's more bellicose rhetoric, Pezeshkian called for ceasefire, highlighting a visible divide between the elected presidency and the Supreme Leader's office.
statements.factCheckDetail: The call for ceasefire is a genuine statement of policy position. The divergence from the Supreme Leader's rhetoric was confirmed by multiple analysts as reflecting a real internal policy disagreement within the Iranian government.

statements.text: Millions of Iranians are displaced and suffering. The humanitarian cost of this conflict demands an immediate end to hostilities.
statements.context: Pezeshkian cited humanitarian concerns as the basis for renewed ceasefire calls, publishing casualty and displacement figures that could not be independently verified.
statements.factCheckDetail: The specific displacement figures cited by Pezeshkian could not be independently verified due to restricted access for international humanitarian organizations. That significant civilian displacement has occurred is widely accepted, but precise numbers remain contested.

statements.text: Any ceasefire must include firm international guarantees that Iran will not be attacked again. We also demand compensation for the assault on our nation and recognition of Iran's right to a full nuclear fuel cycle.
statements.context: Day 12. Pezeshkian publicly laid out Iran's conditions for ending the war, marking the first formal ceasefire demands. Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed back-channel communications with Russia, China, and France. Bloomberg reported the demands are unlikely to be accepted by Washington or Jerusalem.
statements.factCheckDetail: Pezeshkian's conditions confirmed by Bloomberg via officials familiar with the matter; Deputy FM Gharibabadi's statements reported by multiple outlets independently

statements.text: Iran demands three conditions for peace: recognition of our sovereign rights, reparations for the destruction caused by US-Israeli aggression, and binding guarantees against future attacks.
statements.context: First formal articulation of Iran's demands for ending hostilities. Signals Iran open to talks but with high price. Comes as Iran launches largest coordinated attack with Hezbollah.

statements.text: Set terms for ending the war: recognition of Iran's rights, reparations for damage caused, and guarantees against future aggression by the US and Israel.
statements.context: Statement on ceasefire conditions
statements.factCheckNote: Terms reported by Al Jazeera and France 24.

statements.text: Iran has never pursued nuclear arms. We propose the establishment of an Islamic Parliament of the Middle East as a regional security structure.
statements.context: Nowruz message from Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian. Called for regional unity and proposed a new 'Islamic Parliament of the Middle East.' The statement came amid reports that Pezeshkian admitted in closed conversations that he is 'completely excluded from military and strategic decisions.'
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's claim to have never pursued nuclear weapons contradicts longstanding IAEA concerns, enrichment to 60%+ levels, and Netanyahu's claims about nuclear program destruction. However, the specific nuclear fatwa Pezeshkian cites is documented.

statements.text: We do not need the foreigners' presence in the region. We can set up an Islamic assembly in the Middle East with the cooperation of Muslim states, and within that framework, regulate our security, economic, cultural and political relations.
statements.context: Nowruz (Persian New Year) address proposing a new regional security architecture. Pezeshkian called for an "Islamic Assembly of the Middle East" as a framework to replace foreign military presence. He rejected Israel's narrative and called Israel the real source of instability. Also reiterated Iran's rejection of nuclear weapons based on the Supreme Leader's religious fatwa.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI and Tribune India citing Iranian state media broadcast.

statements.text: There needs to be an immediate cessation of US-Israeli aggression. We call on BRICS to play an independent role. We can set up an Islamic Assembly in the Middle East with Muslim states to regulate security, economic, and political relations.
statements.context: Phone call with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Nowruz (Persian New Year). Pezeshkian proposed a regional security framework and called on BRICS nations to act independently of Western pressure.
statements.venue: Phone call with PM Narendra Modi of India
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Al-Monitor and Madhyamam Online reporting on the Modi-Pezeshkian call.

statements.text: You are our brothers, and we have no dispute with you. We seek to live in peace with our neighbors.
statements.context: Eid al-Fitr message to Islamic and neighboring countries on Day 23. Signals Iran is seeking to prevent the conflict from expanding regionally while continuing to fight US and Israel.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Capital News Point and regional media.

statements.text: The illusion of erasing Iran from the map shows desperation against the will of a history-making nation. Threats and terror only strengthen our unity. The Strait of Hormuz is open to all except those who violate our soil. We firmly confront delirious threats on the battlefield.
statements.context: Posted on X in response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening to obliterate Iranian power plants if Hormuz is not opened. Also called on BRICS to 'play an independent role in halting aggressions against Iran.'
statements.factCheckNote: Post confirmed by Tribune India and Al Jazeera Day 23 explainer.

statements.text: The statements of the US president are made in the context of attempts to reduce energy prices and buy time to implement their military plans. There have been no negotiations with Washington.
statements.context: Pezeshkian Telegram post directly responding to Trump's claim of productive US-Iran talks. Called Trump's announcement an attempt to manipulate oil markets.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran denies all contact with US. However, Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt are known intermediaries — whether formal contact occurred through these channels remains disputed.

statements.text: The erosion of the nuclear nonproliferation framework is deeply alarming. The JCPOA remains the best available path to preventing a nuclear crisis.
statements.context: In his annual report on disarmament, Guterres warned about the consequences of the JCPOA's collapse and urged renewed diplomatic engagement.
statements.factCheckDetail: The assessment that the JCPOA framework was eroding is supported by IAEA reports documenting reduced monitoring access and increased Iranian enrichment. The characterization of the JCPOA as the best available path reflects the consensus view of the nonproliferation community.

statements.text: I condemn the Iranian attack on Israel and call on all parties to step back from the brink. The Middle East cannot afford another war.
statements.context: Guterres responded to Iran's direct attack on Israel with a call for de-escalation, attempting to maintain the UN's position of neutrality while condemning the specific act of aggression.
statements.venue: UN Headquarters Press Statement, New York
statements.factCheckDetail: The condemnation and the characterization of the regional security situation are consistent with documented events. The call for restraint represents the official UN position.

statements.text: The failure of diplomacy is not inevitable. It is a choice made by leaders who prefer confrontation to compromise.
statements.context: Guterres expressed growing frustration with the lack of diplomatic engagement between the U.S., Israel, and Iran, placing responsibility on political leaders.
statements.venue: Address to UN General Assembly Special Session
statements.factCheckDetail: This is a normative statement reflecting the Secretary-General's assessment of diplomatic dynamics. While the characterization of diplomatic failure as a political choice is shared by many analysts, others argue structural factors beyond individual leaders' choices contributed to the breakdown.

statements.text: I am deeply concerned by reports of near-weapons-grade enrichment. I urge Iran to cooperate fully with IAEA inspectors.
statements.context: Responding to an IAEA report indicating enrichment levels approaching weapons-grade, Guterres issued a pointed statement calling for transparency.
statements.factCheckDetail: The IAEA did report enrichment levels nearing weapons-grade. Guterres's call for cooperation with inspectors reflected the official UN position and was supported by the IAEA Board of Governors' own resolutions.

statements.text: Military strikes on nuclear facilities risk catastrophic environmental and humanitarian consequences. I call for an immediate cessation of hostilities.
statements.context: Within hours of the first Israeli strikes, Guterres issued an urgent statement warning about the potential for radiological contamination and calling for a ceasefire.
statements.venue: Emergency UN Press Conference, New York
statements.factCheckDetail: The warning about environmental and humanitarian consequences of striking nuclear facilities is supported by scientific assessments from the IAEA and independent nuclear safety experts. Radiological contamination from damaged enrichment facilities is a documented risk.

statements.text: The Security Council's inability to act is a failure of the international system. Civilians are dying while permanent members exercise vetoes.
statements.context: After the U.S. vetoed a ceasefire resolution, Guterres took the unusual step of publicly criticizing the Security Council, reflecting deep frustration with institutional paralysis.
statements.venue: UN General Assembly Emergency Special Session
statements.factCheckDetail: The U.S. did veto a ceasefire resolution. Guterres's characterization of institutional failure reflects factual events. The criticism of the veto mechanism, while politically charged, is grounded in the documented inability of the Council to pass a binding resolution.

statements.text: International humanitarian law applies to all parties in this conflict. Targeting civilian infrastructure is a violation regardless of the justification offered.
statements.context: Guterres responded to reports of strikes hitting power plants, water treatment facilities, and transportation infrastructure in both Iran and Israel.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflects international humanitarian law, specifically the Fourth Geneva Convention and Additional Protocol I, which prohibit attacks on civilian infrastructure. The legal principles cited are well-established in international law.

statements.text: I am appointing a Special Envoy and proposing a 72-hour humanitarian pause. The suffering must stop. We need space for diplomacy.
statements.context: Guterres announced concrete UN-led de-escalation measures, including the appointment of a Special Envoy and a proposed humanitarian corridor agreement.
statements.factCheckDetail: The appointment of a Special Envoy and the proposal for a humanitarian pause are concrete actions within the Secretary-General's authority. Whether parties will accept the proposal remains to be seen.

statements.text: Arrived in Beirut for solidarity visit. The conflict has caused 'immense suffering.' 'De-escalation and dialogue are the only way out.' 773 killed in Lebanon, 800,000+ displaced.
statements.context: Arrival statement in Beirut
statements.factCheckNote: Visit confirmed by UN News. Casualty figures align with known data.

statements.text: Directly addressed the warring parties from Brussels: 'To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control.' Also told Iran: 'Stop attacking your neighbors. They were never parties to the conflict.' Warned the Hormuz closure 'causes enormous pain in so many people around the world that have nothing to do with this conflict.'
statements.context: Briefing European Council leaders in Brussels; strongest UN statement since war began
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera video confirmed; UN News transcript confirmed.

statements.text: Stop attacking your neighbours; they were never parties to the conflict. The world cannot afford a war that spirals completely out of control. The Strait of Hormuz must be opened immediately. The United Nations stands ready to help facilitate safe passage.
statements.context: Guterres addressed the UN Security Council on March 21, calling for Iran to stop attacks on Gulf states and for the US/Israel to halt strikes before the conflict spirals further.
statements.factCheckNote: UN official statement. Confirmed by UN News and UN Press.

statements.text: To the United States and to Israel: it's high time to end this war that is risking getting completely out of control. The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz causes enormous pain in so many people around the world that have nothing to do with this conflict. It's time for the force of the law to prevail over the law of force.
statements.context: Guterres made the remarks outside the European Council meeting in Brussels, calling for an immediate end to the conflict and warning of global economic consequences from the Hormuz closure.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by UN News and Arab News.

speakers.name: Kaja Kallas
speakers.title: EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs

statements.text: Europe must develop an independent strategic posture on Iran. We cannot simply follow Washington's lead on every crisis.
statements.context: As incoming EU High Representative, Kallas signaled a more autonomous European approach to the Iran file, distinguishing EU policy from the incoming Trump administration.
statements.factCheckDetail: The statement accurately reflects the EU's stated intent to maintain strategic autonomy on foreign policy. The EU had already diverged from U.S. Iran policy during the first Trump administration by creating the INSTEX mechanism to preserve trade.

statements.text: The JCPOA was imperfect but it was working. Europe remains committed to a diplomatic solution based on its framework.
statements.context: Kallas reaffirmed European commitment to the diplomatic approach embodied in the JCPOA, contrasting the EU position with the U.S. withdrawal and maximum pressure strategy.
statements.factCheckDetail: The claim that the JCPOA 'was working' is partially supported by IAEA data showing Iran was compliant before the U.S. withdrawal. However, the deal had acknowledged limitations including sunset clauses, and Iran had begun exceeding limits even before full U.S. withdrawal took effect.

statements.text: We have opened a direct diplomatic channel with Tehran. Europe will not stand by while others march toward conflict.
statements.context: Kallas announced EU-Iran diplomatic talks, positioning Europe as an active mediator while the U.S. refused direct engagement with Iran.
statements.factCheckDetail: The EU did establish direct diplomatic contacts with Iran through the EEAS. This was confirmed by both European and Iranian diplomatic sources and represented a significant divergence from U.S. policy.

statements.text: Europe will not participate in or support military action against Iran. We believe this path leads only to catastrophe.
statements.context: As military planning accelerated, Kallas explicitly ruled out European participation, creating a significant rift with the U.S. within the NATO alliance.
statements.factCheckDetail: This accurately represents the official EU position as communicated to NATO allies. The statement created documented tensions within the alliance, with the U.S. expressing disappointment at the lack of allied solidarity.

statements.text: Our mediation efforts have produced a framework for talks. All we need is political will from both sides to take the final step.
statements.context: Kallas revealed that European shuttle diplomacy had produced a draft framework for negotiations, but neither Israel nor Iran had committed to participating.
statements.factCheckDetail: The existence of a draft framework was confirmed by European diplomatic sources but its contents were not made public. Claims of being close to a breakthrough were disputed by both Israeli and U.S. officials who described the framework as inadequate.

statements.text: We condemn these strikes in the strongest possible terms. Unilateral military action undermines international law and regional stability.
statements.context: The EU issued one of the sharpest criticisms of Israeli military action, straining EU-Israel relations and drawing criticism from the U.S. for failing to support an ally.
statements.venue: EU Emergency Foreign Affairs Council Statement
statements.factCheckDetail: The condemnation reflects the official EU position. The legal assessment that unilateral strikes may violate international law is supported by many international law scholars, though Israel disputes this characterization based on self-defense provisions.

statements.text: Europe is imposing targeted sanctions on entities involved in the escalation. We are also tripling our humanitarian aid to affected civilian populations.
statements.context: The EU announced a dual-track response combining punitive measures with humanitarian support, positioning Europe as a distinct voice from both the U.S. and China/Russia.
statements.factCheckDetail: The EU did announce a sanctions package and increased humanitarian aid. The specific aid figure and sanctions targets were detailed in official EU Council documents.

statements.text: We are working with the UN Special Envoy on a European peace initiative. A ceasefire must be the first step, not the last.
statements.context: Kallas announced EU coordination with the newly appointed UN Special Envoy, presenting a joint European-UN diplomatic framework as an alternative to the stalled U.S.-led process.
statements.venue: Joint Press Conference with UN Special Envoy, Geneva
statements.factCheckDetail: EU-UN coordination on a peace initiative was confirmed by both organizations. The existence of a diplomatic framework document was verified, though its prospects for acceptance by the warring parties remained uncertain.

statements.text: We propose a Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz through the UN. Without action, we face food deprivation next year.
statements.context: Kallas proposed a UN-brokered Hormuz shipping arrangement modeled on the 2022 Black Sea Grain Initiative, warning of catastrophic food security consequences if Hormuz remains disrupted.
statements.venue: EU Foreign Affairs Council / Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Kallas's proposal confirmed by Al Jazeera liveblog. The Black Sea grain deal comparison reflects a concrete diplomatic model. Food security warnings align with UN FAO assessments.

statements.text: There is no appetite among European nations for military involvement in the Strait of Hormuz. We propose a Black Sea grain deal model — a humanitarian oil corridor under UN auspices.
statements.context: EU High Representative rejected military involvement in Hormuz, proposing instead a diplomatic framework modeled on the Black Sea grain deal. EU foreign ministers voted against expanding Aspides naval mission.
statements.factCheckNote: EU rejection of Hormuz military role confirmed. Germany's Pistorius explicitly stated 'This is not our war.'

statements.text: We have been consulting with Gulf countries, Jordan, Egypt about whether we could bring forward proposals for Iran, Israel and the US to get out of this situation so that everybody saves face.
statements.context: Kallas called on US and Israel to end the war in a Reuters exclusive. Said EU is consulting regional governments on a face-saving diplomatic exit. Also discussed with UN Secretary-General Guterres a Black Sea grain deal-style arrangement for Hormuz oil transit.
statements.factCheckNote: Reuters exclusive confirmed by Fox News and Al-Monitor. Kallas confirmed consulting with Gulf states on diplomatic off-ramp.

statements.text: There is no appetite in Europe to expand our naval presence or join this military campaign. Europe will not allow itself to be blackmailed.
statements.context: EU summit in Brussels; EU refuses to join US military campaign or secure Hormuz. Kallas refers to Austrian Chancellor Stocker's wording.
statements.factCheckNote: WaPo confirmed

statements.text: Iran bears a heavy responsibility for the current escalation.
statements.context: Statement at UNSC during Resolution 2817 debate. France voted in favor of condemning Iran.
statements.factCheckNote: Official statement at UN, consistent with EU position

statements.text: Member states do not have an appetite to go to this war.
statements.context: After EU Foreign Ministers decided against expanding Aspides naval mission to Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple EU press pool reports

statements.text: EU 'no appetite' to join military campaign. European Council: moratorium on energy/water strikes, reopen Hormuz. ECB: 'material impact on near-term inflation.'
statements.context: European Council summit
statements.factCheckNote: Council conclusions published

statements.text: Iran is responsible for decades of violence and the fewer options Iran has to terrorise the region, the better. The regime is weaker than it has been for many years, but there is no clear trajectory how this war will end. The EU unanimously calls for maximum restraint and de-escalation, and supports reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Statement at EU Brussels summit where all 27 EU heads of state called for a moratorium on energy/water facility strikes and reopening of Strait of Hormuz. EU also approved new sanctions against 19 Iranian officials.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Sofia Globe, US News. EU Council conclusions published.

statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable. Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. We stand firm in calling for an immediate ceasefire and opening of the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Posted on X on Day 23. Kallas also held calls with Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea on Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: EU sanctions vote confirmed. Kallas diplomatic calls confirmed by EEAS.

statements.text: The EU is engaged in intensive diplomacy to address the crisis. I held calls today with Foreign Minister Araghchi, and with counterparts in Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea, covering the conflict, energy infrastructure strikes, and the Hormuz pressure.
statements.context: Kallas provided summary of her diplomatic outreach on March 22, as Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran over Hormuz dominated international attention.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by ANI News reporting on Spanish PM Sanchez and EU diplomatic activity March 22-23.

statements.text: Today, EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations. Iran is an exporter of war. This is not Europe's war, but Europe's interests are directly at stake.
statements.context: EU approved fresh sanctions against 19 Iranian officials and entities. Kallas also announced Rubio will attend G7 foreign ministers meeting in France this week. EU has now sanctioned 263 Iranian individuals and 53 entities.
statements.factCheckNote: EU sanctions confirmed by Kallas X post and EEAS press release.

statements.text: The Islamic Republic will not rest until the aggressors are expelled from our region. My father's blood will not have been spilled in vain.
statements.context: First address as Supreme Leader. 56-year-old mid-ranking religious scholar with close IRGC ties, seen as even more hardline than his father.

statements.text: Iran is prepared for a long war. We will respond to every aggression tenfold. The era of American dominance in our region is over.
statements.context: Statement to CNN via Iranian officials. Putin pledged 'unwavering' support; China opposed targeting the new leader.

statements.text: The Islamic Republic will not rest until every aggressor is punished. Today's integrated operation shows our resolve and capability.
statements.context: Statement attributed to new Supreme Leader via IRGC channels, referencing the joint Iran-Hezbollah barrage. Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly suffered a fractured foot in the initial US-Israeli strikes according to CNN.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz must remain closed as a tool to pressure the enemy. Iran is studying the opening of other fronts. I call on all Iranians to unite behind the Islamic Republic in this sacred defense.
statements.context: First public statement as Supreme Leader, issued via Telegram. His physical condition remains uncertain — reportedly fractured foot and facial injuries from Feb 28 strike.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, Iran International, Gulf News, Euronews

statements.text: Warns attacks on Israel and US assets will continue unless US bases in the region are closed. Describes US Embassy Baghdad strike as part of IRGC's ongoing retaliatory campaign.
statements.context: Statement via IRGC channels
statements.factCheckNote: Statement attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei but delivered through IRGC channels.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz blockade will continue indefinitely. We will decide who passes and who does not. This is Iran's sovereign right.
statements.context: IRGC statement on selective reopening of Hormuz for non-Western ships
statements.factCheckNote: AIS data confirms selective passage being granted to Turkish, Indian, and Saudi vessels

statements.text: Not the right time for peace. Negotiations can only begin once the United States and Israel are defeated and agree to pay compensation.
statements.context: Rejected ceasefire proposals from two intermediary countries
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by multiple outlets

statements.text: Nowruz message: enemies' 'security must be taken away.' Read by TV anchor with still photo — has NOT appeared in person since becoming Supreme Leader.
statements.context: Nowruz statement to Pezeshkian
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed; absence noted by multiple outlets

statements.text: Iran's enemies must have their security taken away. [Iran's response to the killing of intelligence chief Esmail Khatib and other senior officials.]
statements.context: First public statement since becoming Supreme Leader of Iran on Feb 28, 2026 — issued on Nowruz, Day 21 of war
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Al Jazeera, NPR, and Iranian state media

statements.text: Iran's enemies made a gross miscalculation in thinking the Islamic Republic would collapse. They have received a dizzying blow such that they now utter contradictory words and nonsense. Iran will name this year the year of 'resistance economy under national unity and national security.' The attacks attributed to Iran in Turkey and Oman were false flag operations by our enemies. Other fronts may be activated.
statements.context: Full written Nowruz (Persian New Year) statement read by a state television anchor over a still photo — Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since assuming power on Feb 28. The statement claimed enemy 'defeat' while denying Iran's involvement in attacks in Turkey and Oman.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, France 24. Khamenei still not seen publicly.

statements.text: At the moment, due to the particular unity that has been created between you, our compatriots... the enemy has been defeated. The attacks against Türkiye and Oman... were in no way carried out by the armed forces of the Islamic Republic... a ploy by the Zionist enemy, employing the false flag tactic to create discord.
statements.context: Nowruz (Persian New Year) written message read aloud by state TV anchor — no video or audio of Khamenei himself released. Described 2026 as the year of a 'resistance economy under national unity and national security.' Also denied Iranian responsibility for attacks on Turkey and Oman, calling them Israeli false flag operations.
statements.venue: State TV (written message, no public appearance)
statements.factCheckNote: Khamenei's claim that the enemy is 'defeated' is contradicted by ongoing Israeli airstrikes on Tehran. The 'false flag' claim regarding Turkey attacks is disputed: Turkey's MND said NATO intercepted a ballistic missile launched from Iran.

statements.text: There will be no ceasefire. There are no contacts with the United States. Iran will defend itself by any means necessary.
statements.context: Press conference in Tehran.

statements.text: Iran has no interest in negotiations while under attack. Mediation requests from other countries have been rejected.
statements.context: Foreign Ministry spokesperson statement. Despite Oman pushing for mediation and President Pezeshkian acknowledging 'some countries have begun mediation efforts.'

statements.text: We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people. Iran will not accept any ceasefire while our territory is under attack.
statements.context: NBC News interview broadcast March 9. Araghchi rejected ceasefire demands and emphasized Iran's commitment to continued resistance.

statements.text: This is Operation Epic Mistake — a misadventure engineered by Israel and paid for by ordinary Americans. We have very bitter experience with US negotiations. Iran will fight as long as it takes.
statements.context: PBS NewsHour interview. Araghchi called the strikes a failure, ruled out negotiations with the US, and vowed continued missile strikes. Also stated: 'They failed at rapid regime change.'
statements.factCheckDetail: Calling it a 'failure' is contradicted by CENTCOM data showing 5,000+ targets struck and 90% reduction in Iranian missile launches. However, regime change has not occurred and Iran continues retaliating.

statements.text: Iran has now deployed its most powerful weapons including the Khoramshahr super-heavy missile. The enemy should understand this is only the beginning of our response capabilities.
statements.context: Day 12 statement following Iran's 37th wave of attacks using Khoramshahr super-heavy ballistic missiles, Iran's most powerful conventional weapon.

statements.text: We are not asking for ceasefire. Iran's war of resistance will continue until the aggression stops.
statements.context: Statement rejecting ceasefire calls. Contradicts Pezeshkian's peace conditions, aligning more with Mojtaba Khamenei's hardline position.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iranian state media and international outlets

statements.text: UNESCO's silence on the destruction of Iran's historical and cultural landmarks by Israeli bombs is unacceptable. The international community stands complicit.
statements.context: Day 14 statement condemning UNESCO for not speaking out against Israel bombing Iranian cultural sites and landmarks during the campaign.
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's statement confirmed via Iranian state media. UNESCO has not issued a formal response to cultural site damage claims.

statements.text: Mocked US on X: 'After two weeks of war with Iran, White House is now begging the world to buy Russian crude.' Referenced the US waiver of Russia oil sanctions as evidence of economic desperation.
statements.context: Post on X/Twitter
statements.factCheckNote: US did waive Russia oil sanctions. 'Begging' characterization is editorial.

statements.text: Trump is begging others to send warships. This shows the failure of American power. We urge our Gulf neighbors to expel these foreign aggressors from our region.
statements.context: Response to Trump's call for international naval coalition to reopen Hormuz
statements.factCheckNote: Public press conference, statements confirmed by multiple outlets

statements.text: The end of the war depends on guarantees that attacks will not be repeated and payment of compensation for the damage caused during the conflict.
statements.context: Public statement setting conditions for ending hostilities
statements.factCheckNote: Public statement confirmed by multiple outlets.

statements.text: We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans.
statements.context: Time Magazine interview. Araghchi rejected ceasefire negotiations, stating Iran is ready for a long war. Dismissed US-brokered talks entirely.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote confirmed by Time Magazine interview.

statements.text: We don't see any reason why we should talk with Americans. We never asked for a ceasefire.
statements.context: CBS Face the Nation interview. Araghchi reiterated Iran's refusal to negotiate with the US and explicitly stated Iran never requested a ceasefire, contradicting Trump administration claims of Iranian overtures.
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote confirmed by CBS News transcript. Contradicts Trump's claim that Iran was 'begging' for talks.

statements.text: The strait is open, but closed to our enemies, to those who carried out this cowardly aggression against us and to their allies. We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation.
statements.context: Tehran press conference. Also revealed Iran receiving 'military cooperation' from Russia and China.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement broadcast live. Russia-China military cooperation claim is new and significant.

statements.text: Iran has never asked for a ceasefire, and we will not. But this cycle of war must end through negotiations, not through surrender.
statements.context: FM Araghchi addressed reports of ceasefire talks, insisting Iran would not request a ceasefire but remained open to diplomatic resolution. Revealed Russia and China providing 'military cooperation.'
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Iran International and multiple outlets. Iran's refusal to request ceasefire while leaving door open to negotiations reflects a face-saving diplomatic posture.

statements.text: Claims of contact between me and the US envoy are geared solely to mislead oil traders.
statements.context: Publicly denied back-channel communication with US envoy Witkoff, despite Axios report confirming contact resumed
statements.factCheckNote: Multiple sources including Axios confirm contact did occur; Araghchi's denial appears strategic

statements.text: The Islamic Republic has a strong political structure. The presence or absence of a single individual does not affect this structure.
statements.context: On Larijani killing
statements.factCheckNote: Direct quote

statements.text: This war is not our war. We did not start it. The United States started it and is responsible for all consequences.
statements.context: Talk to Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Iran's Hormuz blockade preceded US strikes

statements.text: We don't ask for ceasefire. We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes. The attack on South Pars is a new phase of war.
statements.context: Statement rejecting calls for ceasefire after Gulf energy strikes
statements.factCheckNote: NPR, PressTV confirmed

statements.text: This war is not our war. We did not start it. The United States started it and is responsible for all the consequences. We will end this war when we are certain it will not be repeated. Iran will demand reparations.
statements.context: Al Jazeera interview, March 19. Also stated: 'Naturally we will not allow our enemies to use this waterway' regarding Hormuz.
statements.factCheckNote: Reparations demand is new. Hormuz statement confirms Iran's intent to maintain blockade.

statements.text: Our response to Israel's attack on our infrastructure employed a FRACTION of our power. The ONLY reason for restraint was respect for requested de-escalation. Zero restraint if infrastructure targeted again.
statements.context: Warning after South Pars strikes and Gulf retaliation
statements.factCheckNote: Posted on official Araghchi account

statements.text: Rejected ceasefire: 'We do not want this scenario repeated.' Nuclear material 'under the rubbles' with 'no plan to recover.' Negotiations 'off the table.'
statements.context: Talk to Al Jazeera interview
statements.factCheckNote: Interview broadcast live

statements.text: Signaled possible new rules for navigation through the Strait of Hormuz: rejected ceasefire in favor of a permanent end to attacks. Stated Iran will demand 'assurances that all attacks will stop' and reparations before agreeing to end the conflict. IRGC disputed Netanyahu's claim that Iran cannot build missiles.
statements.context: Day 21 — Iran's defiant posture on Nowruz; IRGC spokesman Naeini made same claim before being killed in airstrike
statements.factCheckNote: Al Jazeera and Times of Israel confirmed IRGC statement and Naeini's death.

statements.text: Our missile industry continues to function at full capacity. Iran's production capabilities have not been degraded. We will continue to respond to every Israeli attack.
statements.context: Statement echoing IRGC spokesman Naini's final claims about missile production; issued after Naini's death
statements.factCheckNote: Claim of unaffected missile production is disputed — Netanyahu said Iran 'can no longer manufacture ballistic missiles'

statements.text: We seek to end the war permanently. This requires a decisive and final end to the attacks — and war reparations. There is no good experience in talking with the Americans, so what good will come of it if we talk again?
statements.context: Araghchi statement reported by Al Arabiya as Iran's first signal of potential war-end conditions. Simultaneously, reports emerged of a CIA-Iranian intelligence backchannel through a third country. Araghchi set two conditions: permanent end to all attacks and reparations. Not yet confirmed by official Iranian state media as of publish time.
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Al Arabiya — not independently confirmed by official Iranian statement as of press time. Described as likely by Al Arabiya's sourcing.

statements.text: We welcome any initiative that can fully end this war; we are ready to listen and consider. However, at present, the United States does not appear ready to halt its aggression. Therefore, we will continue to defend ourselves. We need a permanent, complete, and lasting end — not a temporary ceasefire.
statements.context: Araghchi appeared on NBC's Meet the Press rejecting all ceasefire proposals. He also denied any contact with US envoy Steve Witkoff since the war began: "My last contact with Witkoff was prior to his employer's decision to kill diplomacy with another illegal military attack on Iran." On Hormuz: "The strait is only closed to enemy ships."
statements.factCheckNote: NBC confirmed broadcast. Araghchi's claims about Witkoff contact independently reported by Drop Site News.

statements.text: PM Starmer is putting British lives in danger by allowing UK bases to be used for aggression against Iran. Iran will exercise its right to self-defence.
statements.context: Direct warning to UK Prime Minister Starmer after UK authorized use of Diego Garcia base for US Hormuz strike operations. Warning conveyed on X and separately by phone to UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper.
statements.venue: Post on X and phone call with UK FM Cooper
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News live updates and ITV News reporting.

statements.text: We don't believe in a ceasefire. We believe in ending the war on all fronts — in Lebanon, Yemen, Iraq, Iran and other countries of the region. We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation.
statements.context: Statement rejecting Trump's ceasefire narrative and Araghchi's public response to reports of Iranian backchannel contact with the CIA. Simultaneously demanded reparations for war damages as a precondition for any talks.
statements.venue: NBC News / CBS Face the Nation
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's rejection of ceasefire directly contradicts Trump's claim Iran was "ready to make a deal." Araghchi also separately demanded reparations — confirmed by multiple outlets.

statements.text: Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. No insurer — and no Iranian — will be swayed by more threats. Try respect. Freedom of Navigation cannot exist without Freedom of Trade. Respect both — or expect neither.
statements.context: Posted on X in response to Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum. Also appeared on NBC's Meet the Press rejecting ceasefire calls and confirming Russian support 'in many different directions.' Sent letter to UN Security Council calling power plant threats 'inherently indiscriminate and clearly disproportionate.'
statements.factCheckNote: X post confirmed. NBC Meet the Press interview confirmed. UN letter confirmed by IRNA.

statements.text: Consequences of a US strike on the Bushehr nuclear power plant specifically would be very severe. The international community must understand what is at stake.
statements.context: Warning issued in response to Trump's threat to strike Iranian power plants if Hormuz remains closed. Bushehr NPP contains Russian-supplied nuclear reactors.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill and Times of Israel reporting on Iran's response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum.

statements.text: Attacks on power plants are inherently indiscriminate and clearly disproportionate. I urge the Security Council to compel the aggressors to immediately cease all unlawful attacks and pay reparations. Israel must also place its nuclear facilities under IAEA monitoring.
statements.context: Formal letter sent to UN Security Council documenting Iran's legal case against US-Israeli strikes. Also demanded that the Security Council compel Israel to place its nuclear facilities under IAEA safeguards.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by APA.az and NPR reporting on Araghchi's UN letter.

statements.text: Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. No insurer — and no Iranian — will be swayed by more threats. Try respect.
statements.context: Response to Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum, issued on Day 24 morning via social media.
statements.factCheckNote: Iran maintains Hormuz was declared a "closed military zone" on March 2. CNBC vessel tracking shows ~13 ships/day transiting vs 153+ pre-war — effectively closed regardless of framing.
statements.source: The Hill — Araghchi Iran US conflict

statements.text: My last contact with Mr. Witkoff was prior to his employer's decision to kill diplomacy with another illegal military attack on Iran. Any claim to the contrary appears geared solely to mislead oil traders and the public. There is no direct or indirect contact with the United States.
statements.context: Araghchi's categorical denial of Trump's Truth Social claim of 'very good and productive conversations' with Iran. Issued hours after Trump's 5-day pause announcement. Iran's Fars News cited an unnamed government source confirming no US contact.
statements.venue: X (Twitter) post / Iranian Foreign Ministry statement
statements.factCheckNote: Araghchi's denial confirmed by Republic World, Euronews liveblog, and Al Jazeera. Iran's Fars News independently confirmed 'no direct or indirect contact.' Sharp contradiction with Trump's claim of productive talks.
statements.source: Republic World — Iran rejects Trump's productive talks claim

statements.text: Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. Freedom of Navigation cannot exist without Freedom of Trade. Respect both — or expect neither.
statements.context: Araghchi X post pushing back on Trump's Hormuz ultimatum framing. Part of Iran's broader diplomatic counter-narrative on Day 24.
statements.factCheckNote: While technically the strait is not formally closed, AIS data shows less than 2% of pre-war transit levels — the IEA has characterized it as the largest oil supply disruption in modern history. Iran's mines and active IRGC deterrence are the primary cause of the shipping halt, not insurer decisions alone.

statements.text: Iran has the approval and authority to close this issue quickly if our conditions are met. But no agreement has been reached. Significant gaps remain.
statements.context: Reported private signal to US envoys Witkoff and Kushner per Iran International. Araghchi reportedly cited approval from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Simultaneously, Iran's public statements denied all negotiations.
statements.venue: Private communication with US envoys (reported by Iran International)
statements.factCheckNote: Reported by Iran International citing sources. Iran's public statements directly contradict — Ghalibaf called talks claims fabricated. Dual-track public/private diplomacy is consistent with historical Iranian negotiating patterns.

statements.text: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed to vessels linked to the United States and Israeli military aggression. This is a direct result of American and Israeli military aggression against the Iranian people.
statements.context: Araghchi's public statement to Malaysia's FM maintained Hormuz closure while Iran International simultaneously reported Araghchi privately told Witkoff and Kushner that Mojtaba Khamenei approved deal talks if conditions are met.
statements.factCheckNote: Hormuz closure is confirmed fact. Dual-track (public hardline / private signals) is a consistent Iranian diplomatic pattern.

statements.text: I urge an immediate end to violence in Iran and across the Middle East. War is always a defeat for humanity.
statements.context: Address from Vatican.

statements.text: I call for the roar of bombs to cease. Humanity must choose dialogue over destruction.
statements.context: Vatican Angelus address. Called for 'a space for dialogue to open.' Tone described as 'relatively restrained' compared to John Paul II on Iraq War.

statements.text: I express deep sorrow for all those killed and wounded. The tragedy unfolding must stop before it becomes an irreparable abyss.
statements.context: New Vatican appeal March 10. Cardinal McElroy (Washington) stated the war does not meet 'minimum criteria' for being morally just. Pope Leo XIV has become a leading voice calling for cessation of hostilities.

statements.text: Cease fire!
statements.context: Strongest papal appeal yet on the Iran conflict. Pope Leo XIV directly addressed war leaders and referenced the school strike that killed 165+ children. Described as the most forceful Vatican intervention since John Paul II on Iraq.

statements.text: In the name of God, I ask: stop the bombing. Stop killing children. The school strike that took 165 young lives cries out to heaven.
statements.context: Sunday noon blessing from Vatican. Pope Leo XIV delivered his strongest ceasefire appeal yet, directly referencing the school strike that killed 165+ children. Called on all parties to begin dialogue immediately.
statements.venue: Sunday Noon Blessing, St. Peter's Square, Vatican
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Vatican News. The school strike reference aligns with confirmed reporting on the Minab girls' school attack.

statements.text: On behalf of the Christians of the Middle East and all women and men of good will, I appeal to those responsible for this conflict. Cease fire so that avenues for dialogue may be reopened.
statements.context: Sunday noon blessing. Strongest statement yet. Vatican Secretary of State Cardinal Parolin rejected 'preventive war' claim. US Cardinals McElroy and Cupich also publicly criticized the war.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by UPI, Washington Post, and Newsweek

statements.text: Cease the fire! In the name of God, I beg you — cease the fire. Every bomb that falls, every missile that strikes, kills a child of God.
statements.context: Pope Leo XIV's third and most forceful appeal for ceasefire, delivered during Sunday Angelus address. Vatican confirmed direct phone calls to both Trump and Mojtaba Khamenei.
statements.factCheckNote: Vatican Press Office confirmed the Pope's direct calls to both heads of state. This represents an unprecedented level of papal diplomatic engagement.

statements.text: Cardinal Parolin: Would urge Trump 'to stop as soon as possible, danger of escalation imminent.' Rejected 'preventive war' justification.
statements.context: Vatican Secretary of State remarks
statements.factCheckNote: CathNews, NCR confirmed

statements.text: Stability and peace are not achieved through mutual threats, nor through the use of weapons, which sow destruction, suffering, and death, but only through reasonable, sincere, and responsible dialogue. Persevere in prayer, so that hostilities may cease and paths of peace may finally open up.
statements.context: Sunday Angelus address at St. Peter's Square, Rome. Pope Leo XIV's second personal appeal for ceasefire since the Iran War began. He said he continues to follow the situation 'with dismay.' Trump responded hours later by rejecting the appeal.
statements.venue: Sunday Angelus, St. Peter's Square, Vatican
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by The Hill, ABC7, and PhilNews reporting on Pope Leo XIV's March 22 Angelus address.

statements.text: On behalf of the Christians of the Middle East and all women and men of good will, I appeal to those responsible for this conflict. Cease fire so that avenues for dialogue may be reopened. Violence can never lead to the justice, stability, and peace that the people are waiting for. Some claim to involve the name of God in these deadly decisions, but God cannot be enlisted by darkness.
statements.context: Strongest papal ceasefire appeal to date, delivered during Sunday March 22 Angelus address. Referenced missile strike on Iranian elementary school. Did not name US or Israel directly. Trump responded: "I don't want a ceasefire."
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Vatican News and multiple Catholic media outlets.
statements.source: Vatican News — Pope Leo ceasefire appeal

statements.text: The attacks on Iran constitute violations of international humanitarian law. Switzerland has a duty to speak.
statements.context: Official government statement.

statements.text: The death of a French soldier in Iraq is unacceptable. France is present in Iraq solely for counterterrorism. We demand a full investigation and accountability.
statements.context: First French military fatality of the conflict. A French soldier was killed in an Iraqi militia drone attack on a French counterterrorism base. Macron called it 'unacceptable' and demanded accountability, raising concerns about NATO expansion of the conflict.
statements.factCheckNote: French soldier death confirmed by French Ministry of Armed Forces and France24. France maintains counterterrorism forces in Iraq under Operation Chammal.

statements.text: France would never take part in operations to unblock the Strait of Hormuz in the current context.
statements.context: Macron explicitly ruled out French participation in any US-led military operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, joining other European nations in rejecting Trump's demands for allied help.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by CBS News and Al Jazeera liveblog coverage of EU foreign ministers' decisions.

statements.text: This conflict will certainly come to an end in the next few weeks.
statements.context: ABC 'This Week' interview. Energy Secretary Wright predicted the war would end within weeks, suggesting the administration sees a timeline for resolution. Comments come amid sustained $100+ oil prices and growing economic pressure.
statements.factCheckNote: Prediction about conflict duration. IDF Brig Gen Defrin separately indicated plans through Passover (3+ more weeks), contradicting a quick resolution.

statements.text: We have plans through Passover. Over 2,000 targets have been hit. 70% of Iran's missile launchers have been destroyed. More than 6,000 IRGC fighters have been killed.
statements.context: IDF Brig Gen Effie Defrin outlined the scope and timeline of Israeli operations against Iran. The Passover timeline (April 12) implies at least 3 more weeks of operations. The 6,000+ IRGC killed claim is significantly higher than Iranian government figures.
statements.factCheckNote: Strike numbers plausible given CENTCOM data. IRGC casualty claim of 6,000+ cannot be independently verified due to Iran's internet blackout. Iran's Health Ministry reports 1,444 total killed.

statements.text: We have not yet made any decisions regarding the dispatch of naval vessels.
statements.context: Responding to Trump's call for allied nations to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz. Japan, which imports ~90% of its oil through the strait, faces immense pressure but Takaichi signaled caution amid domestic opposition to military involvement.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Japan Times and Bloomberg. Japan's constitutional constraints on military deployment make this a sensitive domestic issue.

statements.text: The United States will allow Iranian oil tankers to transit through the Strait of Hormuz. We are working to ensure global energy markets are not destabilized beyond what is necessary.
statements.context: Major policy shift. Treasury Secretary announced US would not block Iranian tankers in Hormuz. Oil dropped 4-6% immediately. WSJ reports US simultaneously building multinational naval escort coalition for commercial shipping.
statements.factCheckNote: Oil markets confirmed the impact: Brent -4.3% to $100.21, WTI -6.1% to $93.50. However, the strait remains effectively closed to most commercial traffic.

statements.text: Turkey condemns the expansion of Israeli military operations into Lebanon. This aggressive action threatens the entire region's stability.
statements.context: Turkey condemned IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon. Turkey has been allowing Iranian ships through selective Hormuz passage while maintaining NATO membership, walking a delicate line.
statements.factCheckNote: Turkish MFA issued formal condemnation. Turkey's position is complicated by NATO membership and economic ties to both sides.

statements.text: Ali Larijani, former Speaker of Iran's Parliament and senior adviser to the Supreme Leader, was eliminated in a targeted strike. This sends a clear message — no one in the regime is beyond our reach.
statements.context: Israel confirmed the targeted killing of Ali Larijani, one of the most senior Iranian political figures killed in the conflict. Larijani was reportedly en route to a meeting with Mojtaba Khamenei.
statements.venue: Official statement, Israeli Ministry of Defense
statements.factCheckNote: Larijani's death confirmed by multiple Iranian and international sources. Iran's IRNA and Tasnim news agencies confirmed the killing.

statements.text: This week, the intensity of the attacks that the IDF and the U.S. military will carry out against the Iranian terrorist regime and against the infrastructures on which it relies will increase significantly, and the campaign led by U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will continue.
statements.context: Defense Minister Katz statement came within hours of Trump's "winding down" Truth Social post, directly contradicting the US president's signals. Katz also said Israel is "determined to continue leading the attack against the Iranian terrorist regime, to behead its commanders and thwart its strategic capabilities — until all security threats to the State of Israel and U.S. interests in the region are removed."
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by US News/AP and TASS.

statements.text: If this continues, we'll be sure to hit Iran so hard it will be sent back decades. The IRGC intentionally fired at population centers. The intensity of the strikes to be carried out by the IDF and the US against the Iranian terror regime will rise significantly.
statements.context: Statement at Arad after the Day 22 Iranian IRBM direct hit that injured 74 people, including threat to intensify attacks. Also announced IDF strikes on all Litani River bridges in Lebanon.
statements.factCheckNote: Katz statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. Litani bridge strikes order confirmed by IDF.

statements.text: All bridges over the Litani River used by Hezbollah have been destroyed. Any village serving as a terrorist outpost will be treated like Rafah and Beit Hanoun.
statements.context: Israeli Defense Minister announcing implementation of the 'Rafah and Beit Hanoun models' for Lebanese villages used as Hezbollah bases. Made alongside announcement of 7 overnight Beirut airstrikes.
statements.factCheckNote: Litani bridge destruction confirmed by satellite imagery and IDF statements. Threat of Rafah model application made explicitly by Katz.

statements.text: The assassination of Larijani is a war crime that will not go unanswered. The cycle of war must end, but not through the murder of our statesmen.
statements.context: Parliament Speaker Qalibaf condemned the targeted killing of Ali Larijani, calling for both retaliation and an end to the cycle of violence. His dual message reflects Iran's internal tension between escalation and negotiation.
statements.factCheckNote: Emergency session confirmed by Iranian state media. Qalibaf's statement represents the legislative branch's first formal response to the Larijani killing.

statements.text: The threat of war against Iran and the region must be eliminated. We will not accept the cycle of war, ceasefire, negotiation, and war again.
statements.context: Parliament address after Larijani killing
statements.factCheckNote: PressTV confirmed statement

statements.text: The skies of Israel are unprotected. The pre-prepared plans of the Islamic Republic must be implemented now.
statements.context: Statement from Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf after Iranian ballistic missile successfully struck Dimona, evading Israeli air defenses — the first strike in proximity to Israel's nuclear research center.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Haaretz live blog Day 22 coverage of Dimona strike.

statements.text: If the US and Israel target Iranian power plants, the energy infrastructure and desalination facilities in the region will be irreversibly destroyed. This will push oil prices higher for a long time.
statements.context: Iranian Parliament Speaker warning issued in response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening to strike Iranian power plants if Hormuz is not reopened.
statements.factCheckNote: Statement confirmed by Al Jazeera and multiple outlets covering Iranian response to Trump ultimatum.
statements.source: Al Jazeera — Iran threatens energy sites if US Israel target power plants

statements.text: No negotiations have been held with the United States. Trump's claims are an attempt to manipulate the financial and oil markets. The Islamic Republic has not entered into any negotiations.
statements.context: Iranian Parliament Speaker response to Trump's public announcement of US-Iran talks. Simultaneously, Iran International reported Ghalibaf himself was the Iranian official in backchannel contact with Witkoff/Kushner via Pakistani intermediaries.
statements.factCheckNote: Ghalibaf publicly denied negotiations, but Iran International reported he was himself the contact relayed through Pakistani intermediaries. The contradiction is notable and suggests deliberate public denial alongside backchannel engagement.

statements.text: We need to find an off-ramp. The economic damage from this conflict — oil prices, shipping disruptions, market instability — is becoming unsustainable for the global economy.
statements.context: David Sacks, Trump's AI and crypto policy adviser, publicly broke from hawkish administration messaging to call for diplomatic off-ramp. His comments reflect growing concern among economic advisers about the war's financial toll.
statements.factCheckNote: Sacks' public call for an off-ramp was notable as the first senior White House official to publicly advocate for de-escalation during the conflict.

statements.text: Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.
statements.context: Resignation letter — became highest-profile Trump official to quit over the war. 11-time combat veteran, Gold Star spouse.
statements.factCheckNote: Kent's claim about motivation is his opinion; the imminent threat assessment is debated by intelligence community

statements.text: The President concluded that the terrorist Islamist regime in Iran posed an imminent threat and he took action. He is responsible for determining what is and is not an imminent threat.
statements.context: First public statement on the war — did not personally endorse the war, only said the president determines threat level
statements.factCheckNote: Gabbard confirmed statement; notable for NOT endorsing war given her 'No War With Iran' history

statements.text: Yes there are potential leaders in Iran today who can lead the nation in the right direction, use the resources for prosperity, for infrastructure and not to send ballistic missiles to all its neighbors and block the Strait of Hormuz.
statements.context: Danon appeared on CNN and said there are alternative Iranian leaders who could open the door to diplomacy. Declined to name specific individuals but signaled Israel has identified potential negotiating partners.
statements.factCheckNote: C-SPAN and CNN confirmed Danon's remarks. First explicit Israeli acknowledgment of potential diplomatic partners inside Iran.

speakers.name: Kevin Hassett
speakers.title: Director of the National Economic Council

statements.text: If the war were to be extended, it wouldn't really disrupt the US economy very much at all. [Consumer pain] is the last of our concerns right now.
statements.context: CNBC interview. Hassett said consumer pain from rising gas prices was 'the last of our concerns,' sparking fierce backlash from Democrats. Rep. Ted Lieu said he wanted Hassett sent to every swing district to repeat the statement.
statements.factCheckNote: Gas has risen 88 cents/gallon in a month to $3.80, diesel up $1.39 to $5.04. Consumer pain is measurable and growing.

statements.text: This year's celebrations come under different circumstances. The country is effectively in a state of war. Supporters of the Islamic Republic must not leave the squares empty.
statements.context: Police Commander Radan warned of crackdown ahead of Chaharshanbe Suri fire festival. Government sent mass text warnings that 'unconventional behaviour' could result in imprisonment and flogging.
statements.factCheckNote: Confirmed by Iran International and IranWire reporting.

statements.text: Bombing Iran will not achieve regime change. We risk a perpetual war. NATO is a defensive alliance, not an offensive one.
statements.context: Shift from initial support to open criticism of war
statements.factCheckNote: DW News confirmed
